sources.title: SEC. 31-101. - TITLE.
sources.excerpt: This Ordinance shall be known and may be cited as the City of Suffolk, Virginia Unified Development Ordinance (hereinafter "the Ordinance"). This Ordinance may also be known, and may be referred to, as the "UDO."
sources.full_text: This Ordinance shall be known and may be cited as the City of Suffolk, Virginia Unified Development Ordinance (hereinafter "the Ordinance"). This Ordinance may also be known, and may be referred to, as the "UDO."

sources.title: SEC. 31-102. - PURPOSE.
sources.excerpt: The Ordinance is intended to enable the City to respond uniformly and consistently to development proposals and to promote the health, safety and general welfare of the residents throughout the entire City, while providing uniformity, certainty and predictability for persons subject to this Ordinance. While the majority of situations subject to this Ordinance will fall into easily identifiable processes and requirements, there has been an attempt to provide flexibility in dealing with situations
sources.full_text: The Ordinance is intended to enable the City to respond uniformly and consistently to development proposals and to promote the health, safety and general welfare of the residents throughout the entire City, while providing uniformity, certainty and predictability for persons subject to this Ordinance. While the majority of situations subject to this Ordinance will fall into easily identifiable processes and requirements, there has been an attempt to provide flexibility in dealing with situations which may fall outside these typical processes and requirements. The elements that make up the Ordinance are interrelated and cannot be taken in isolation; they must be taken within the context and intent of the entire Ordinance. Specifically, the purposes of this Ordinance are as described in subsections (a) through (c) of this section. (a) Pursuant to Code of Virginia, § 15.2-2200, it is the purpose of this Ordinance to improve the public health, safety, convenience and welfare of the citizens of the City of Suffolk, to plan for the future development of communities to the end that transportation systems be carefully planned; to provide for new community centers to be developed with adequate highway, utility, health, educational, and recreational facilities; to recognize the need for mineral resources and the needs of agriculture, industry and business be recognized in future growth; to provide residential areas with healthy surroundings for family life; to preserve agricultural and forestal land; and to provide for the growth of the community consonant with the efficient and economical use of public funds. (b) Zoning regulations are included in Articles 4, 6, 7 and 8. Pursuant to Code of Virginia § 15.2-2283, the power of zoning is exercised in order to: (1) Implement the Comprehensive Plan; (2) To promote the health, safety or general welfare of the public and of further accomplishing this Ordinance; (3) To provide for adequate light, air, convenience of access, and safety from fire, flood, crime and other dangers; (4) To reduce or prevent congestion in the public streets; (5) To facilitate the creation of a convenient, attractive and harmonious community; (6) To facilitate the provision of adequate police and fire protection, disaster evacuation, civil defense, transportation, water, sewerage, flood protection, schools, parks, forests, playgrounds, recreational facilities, airports and other public requirements; (7) To protect against destruction of or encroachment upon historic areas; (8) To provide for conveyance, in appropriate cases, of common or shared easements to franchised cable television operators furnishing cable television and public service corporations furnishing cable television, gas, telephone and electric service to the proposed subdivision; (9) To provide for monuments of specific types to be installed establishing street and property lines; (10) To provide for the filing of plats for recordation and the expiration of unrecorded plats; (11) To provide for the administration and enforcement of this Ordinance; (12) To provide for single divisions of a lot or parcel for the purpose of sale or gift to a member of the immediate family of the property owner; (13) To provide for variations in or exceptions to the general regulations of the subdivision provisions of this Ordinance in cases of unusual situations or when strict adherence to the general regulations would result in substantial injustice or hardship; (14) To impose a requirement for the furnishing of a preliminary opinion from the applicable health official regarding the suitability of a subdivision for installation of subsurface sewage disposal systems where such method of sewage disposal is to be utilized in the development of a subdivision; (15) To provide requirements that, in the event streets in a subdivision will not be constructed to meet the standards necessary for inclusion in the secondary system of state highways or for state street maintenance moneys paid to municipalities, the subdivision plat and all approved deeds of subdivision, or similar instruments, must contain a statement advising that the streets in the subdivision do not meet state standards and will not be maintained by the City; and (16) To provide for the voluntary funding of off-site road improvements and reimbursements of advances by the City Council. (Ord. No. 19-O-108, § 2(Exh. B), 10-16-2019)

sources.title: SEC. 31-103. - AUTHORITY.
sources.excerpt: The provisions of this Ordinance are authorized by Chapter 22, Title 15.2 of the Code of Virginia.
sources.full_text: The provisions of this Ordinance are authorized by Chapter 22, Title 15.2 of the Code of Virginia.

sources.title: SEC. 31-104. - APPLICABILITY.
sources.excerpt: (a) GENERALLY. The Ordinance shall appply to all public land(s) and building(s) and private land(s) and building(s), and use(s) thereon over which the City has jurisdiction under the Constitution(s) and law(s) of the State of Virginia and of the United States. (b) CERTAIN UTILITIES AND SERVICES EXEMPT. Except as specifically noted below, certain utilities and services shall be exempt from the regulations of this Ordinance. Specifically, the following facilities and equipment shall be exempted: (
sources.full_text: (a) GENERALLY. The Ordinance shall appply to all public land(s) and building(s) and private land(s) and building(s), and use(s) thereon over which the City has jurisdiction under the Constitution(s) and law(s) of the State of Virginia and of the United States. (b) CERTAIN UTILITIES AND SERVICES EXEMPT. Except as specifically noted below, certain utilities and services shall be exempt from the regulations of this Ordinance. Specifically, the following facilities and equipment shall be exempted: (1) Traffic signals, fire hydrants, alarm or emergency devices, telephone booths and pedestals, mailboxes; (2) Wires, poles, towers, pipes, meters and similar facilities which provide service connections between primary distribution lines or mains and individual residential, commercial or industrial customers, or which are an integral and accessory part of a subdivision or development excluding any telecommunications facilities regulated by the Telecommunications Facilities Standards of this Ordinance. (3) Sewage pump and lift stations, water storage and pumping facilities, communication switching and relay facilities, and similar utilities when approved by the Director as a necessary and integral component of a public utility system. Such facilities shall be subject to the terms of Article 6. (4) Railroad tracks, signals, bridges and similar facilities and equipment located on a railroad right-of-way, and maintenance and repair work on such facilities and equipment. (5) Towers or antennas used primarily bv governmental agencies for public safety communications and emergency services. (6) Security fencing for public utility facilities (generation, transmission, treatment, distribution and communication). No such fence when located in a required yard adjacent to a street shall obstruct traffic sight visibility as determined by the City's Traffic Engineer. (7) The installation, placement, maintenance, or replacement of micro-wireless facilities that are suspended on cables or lines that are strung between existing utility poles in compliance with national safety codes. (c) Any utility substation, treatment plant, generating plant, or similar facility which is not within the normal scope of distribution facilities referred to above shall be authorized only by conditional use permit. (Ord. No. 13-O-068, 7-17-2013; Ord. No. 15-O-021, 3-4-2015; Ord. No. 17-O-055, § 2(Exh. B), 6-21-2017)

sources.title: SEC. 31-105. - CONSISTENCY WITH COMPREHENSIVE PLAN.
sources.excerpt: (a) Pursuant to Code of Virginia, § 15.2-2284, this Ordinance is intended to implement the themes, policies, actions and maps of the Comprehensive Plan and is hereby deemed to be consistent and in accordance with the adopted Comprehensive Plan. Any amendments to or actions pursuant to the Ordinance shall be consistent with the applicable Comprehensive Plan, as it may be amended from time to time. (b) An amendment to the text of the Ordinance is consistent with and in accordance with the Comprehe
sources.full_text: (a) Pursuant to Code of Virginia, § 15.2-2284, this Ordinance is intended to implement the themes, policies, actions and maps of the Comprehensive Plan and is hereby deemed to be consistent and in accordance with the adopted Comprehensive Plan. Any amendments to or actions pursuant to the Ordinance shall be consistent with the applicable Comprehensive Plan, as it may be amended from time to time. (b) An amendment to the text of the Ordinance is consistent with and in accordance with the Comprehensive Plan if it complies with the themes, policies, actions and maps stated in the Comprehensive Plan, as it may be amended from time to time. An amendment to the zoning map is consistent with the Comprehensive Plan if it complies with the themes, policies, actions and maps stated in the Comprehensive Plan, as it may be amended from time to time. (Ord. No. 18-O-058, § 2(Exh. B), 6-20-2018; Ord. No. 25-O-053, § 2(Exh. B), 5-21-2025)

sources.title: SEC. 31-106. - COORDINATION WITH OTHER REGULATIONS.
sources.excerpt: (a) The use of buildings and land within the City shall be subject to all other regulations as well as this Ordinance, whether or not such other provisions are specifically referenced in this Ordinance. References to other regulations or provisions of the Ordinance are for the convenience of the reader; lack of a cross reference should not be construed as an indication that other regulations do not apply. (b) In interpreting and applying the provisions of this Ordinance, they shall be construed
sources.full_text: (a) The use of buildings and land within the City shall be subject to all other regulations as well as this Ordinance, whether or not such other provisions are specifically referenced in this Ordinance. References to other regulations or provisions of the Ordinance are for the convenience of the reader; lack of a cross reference should not be construed as an indication that other regulations do not apply. (b) In interpreting and applying the provisions of this Ordinance, they shall be construed to be the minimum requirements necessary for the promotion of public health, safety or the general welfare. Minimum values are not intended to be target values. In some instances, conditions may create the need to exceed stated minimum standards. Whenever this Ordinance requires, for example, a lower height of a building or lesser number of stories, or requires a greater percentage of the lot to be left unoccupied, or imposes more restrictive standards than are required pursuant to any other statute or local regulation, this Ordinance shall govern.

sources.title: SEC. 31-107. - TRANSITIONAL PROVISIONS.
sources.excerpt: (a) VIOLATIONS CONTINUE. Any violation of the previous Ordinance will continue to be a violation under this Ordinance and be subject to penalties and enforcement under Section 31-310 of this Ordinance, unless the use, development, construction or other activity complies with the provisions of this Ordinance. (b) NONCONFORMITIES UNDER PRIOR ORDINANCE. Any legal nonconformity under a previous zoning or subdivision Ordinance also will be a legal nonconformity under this Ordinance, so long as the si
sources.full_text: (a) VIOLATIONS CONTINUE. Any violation of the previous Ordinance will continue to be a violation under this Ordinance and be subject to penalties and enforcement under Section 31-310 of this Ordinance, unless the use, development, construction or other activity complies with the provisions of this Ordinance. (b) NONCONFORMITIES UNDER PRIOR ORDINANCE. Any legal nonconformity under a previous zoning or subdivision Ordinance also will be a legal nonconformity under this Ordinance, so long as the situation that resulted in the legal nonconforming status under the previous Ordinance or under this Ordinance continues to exist. If a legal nonconformity under the previous Ordinance becomes conforming because of the adoption of this Ordinance, then said use or structure will no longer be considered a nonconformity. (c) CONDITIONAL REZONING. The classification of a parcel pursuant to the Official Zoning Map of this Ordinance shall not constitute an amendment or variation of any conditions created pursuant to a conditional rezoning approved prior to the effective date of this Ordinance. The landowner or applicant for development approval shall remain subject to all such conditions unless amended pursuant to Code of Virginia, § 15.2-2302.

sources.title: SEC. 31-108. - INTERPRETATION.
sources.excerpt: (a) Interpretation and application of the provisions of this Ordinance shall be regarded as the basic and minimum requirements for the protection of public health, safety, comfort, morals, convenience, prosperity and welfare. To the extent permitted by Virginia law, this Ordinance shall be liberally interpreted in order to further its underlying purposes. (b) Whenever any provision of the Ordinance or any provision of any other applicable law, rule, contract, resolution or regulation of the City
sources.full_text: (a) Interpretation and application of the provisions of this Ordinance shall be regarded as the basic and minimum requirements for the protection of public health, safety, comfort, morals, convenience, prosperity and welfare. To the extent permitted by Virginia law, this Ordinance shall be liberally interpreted in order to further its underlying purposes. (b) Whenever any provision of the Ordinance or any provision of any other applicable law, rule, contract, resolution or regulation of the City, state agency, or the federal government contains certain standards, covering the same subject matter, the more restrictive requirement(s) or higher standard(s) shall control unless otherwise provided herein. The meaning of any and all words, terms or phrases in this Ordinance shall be construed in accordance with Appendix A hereto, which is hereby incorporated by reference as if set forth in its entirety herein, and any particular definitions assigned by any Article or Section of this Ordinance. (c) This Ordinance contains a number of illustrations and graphics which are designed to assist the reader in understanding the provisions of this Ordinance. However, to the extent that there is any inconsistency between the text of this Ordinance and any illustration of graphics, the text shall control.

sources.title: SEC. 31-109. - SEVERABILITY.
sources.excerpt: It is hereby declared to be the intent of the City that the provisions of this Ordinance shall be severable. If any provision is declared invalid by a court of competent jurisdiction, it is hereby declared to be the legislative intent that the effect of such decision shall be limited to that provision or provisions which are expressly stated in the decision to be invalid; and such decision shall not affect, impair or nullify this Ordinance as a whole or any other part thereof, but the rest of th
sources.full_text: It is hereby declared to be the intent of the City that the provisions of this Ordinance shall be severable. If any provision is declared invalid by a court of competent jurisdiction, it is hereby declared to be the legislative intent that the effect of such decision shall be limited to that provision or provisions which are expressly stated in the decision to be invalid; and such decision shall not affect, impair or nullify this Ordinance as a whole or any other part thereof, but the rest of the Ordinance shall continue in full force and effect.

sources.title: SEC. 31-110. - PERMITS AND CERTIFICATES.
sources.excerpt: No development activity shall occur on any property within the jurisdiction of this Ordinance until all applicable permits, approvals and certificates have been issued and approved by the officials with the authority to approve the same pursuant to Article 3 of this Ordinance.
sources.full_text: No development activity shall occur on any property within the jurisdiction of this Ordinance until all applicable permits, approvals and certificates have been issued and approved by the officials with the authority to approve the same pursuant to Article 3 of this Ordinance.

sources.title: SEC. 31-111. - FEES.
sources.excerpt: The City Council may, by ordinance, establish administrative fees considered necessary to enforce and administer the Ordinance pursuant to Code of Virginia, § 15.2-107. Such fees shall be limited to the reasonable costs of administering and processing applications for development approval. No permit shall be processed, and no permit shall be considered to be submitted, until all applicable administrative fees have been paid pursuant to this Section and any Ordinance adopted hereto.
sources.full_text: The City Council may, by ordinance, establish administrative fees considered necessary to enforce and administer the Ordinance pursuant to Code of Virginia, § 15.2-107. Such fees shall be limited to the reasonable costs of administering and processing applications for development approval. No permit shall be processed, and no permit shall be considered to be submitted, until all applicable administrative fees have been paid pursuant to this Section and any Ordinance adopted hereto.

sources.title: SEC. 31-112. - DELINQUENT REAL ESTATE TAXES AND OTHER PROPERTY LIENS.
sources.excerpt: Prior to acceptance of an Application for a Development Approval in accordance with § 15.2-2286 of the Code of Virginia, as amended, the City shall require the applicant to produce satisfactory evidence that any delinquent real estate taxes, nuisance charges, stormwater management utility fees, and any other charges that constitute a lien on the subject property that are owed to the City and which have been properly assessed against the subject property have been paid, unless otherwise authorize
sources.full_text: Prior to acceptance of an Application for a Development Approval in accordance with § 15.2-2286 of the Code of Virginia, as amended, the City shall require the applicant to produce satisfactory evidence that any delinquent real estate taxes, nuisance charges, stormwater management utility fees, and any other charges that constitute a lien on the subject property that are owed to the City and which have been properly assessed against the subject property have been paid, unless otherwise authorized by the Treasurer. (Ord. No. 12-O-101, 12-5-2012; Ord. No. 17-O-055, § 2(Exh. B), 6-21-2017) Editor's note— Ord. No. 12-O-101, adopted Dec. 5, 2012, changed the title of § 31-112 from "Delinquent Real Estate Taxes" to "Delinquent Real Estate Taxes and Other Property Liens."

sources.title: SEC. 31-113. - EFFECTIVE DATE.
sources.excerpt: This Ordinance and any amendment thereto shall become effective September 7, 1999; provided, however, that the provisions of Section 31-601 of this Ordinance shall become effective on July 1, 2000.
sources.full_text: This Ordinance and any amendment thereto shall become effective September 7, 1999; provided, however, that the provisions of Section 31-601 of this Ordinance shall become effective on July 1, 2000.

sources.title: SEC. 31-114. - EFFECT OF ORDINANCE.
sources.excerpt: The policies and regulations in this Ordinance apply to the use and development of land. No application for development approval shall be approved by any officer, official or agency of the City unless said application conforms to and complies with the procedures, standards and criteria of this Ordinance. Article 2 establishes and describes the agencies, public officials and public bodies involved in the development approval process. Detailed information about the general procedures and applicati
sources.full_text: The policies and regulations in this Ordinance apply to the use and development of land. No application for development approval shall be approved by any officer, official or agency of the City unless said application conforms to and complies with the procedures, standards and criteria of this Ordinance. Article 2 establishes and describes the agencies, public officials and public bodies involved in the development approval process. Detailed information about the general procedures and applications, methods of appeal and violations are discussed in Article 3. Permitted uses are set forth in Article 4. Provisions pertaining to the subdivision of land are established in Article 5. General standards and criteria are set forth in Article 6. Special regulations applicable to particular uses are set forth in Article 7. Procedures and standards for nonconforming uses and vested rights are set forth in Article 8.

sources.title: SEC. 31-201. - PLANNING AND COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT DEPARTMENT AND ZONING ADMINISTRATOR.
sources.excerpt: (a) ESTABLISHMENT OF PLANNING AND COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT DEPARTMENT. The City Council hereby establishes and reaffirms the existence of the Planning and Community Development Department of the City of Suffolk. The Planning and Community Development Department shall perform the planning functions for the City and shall provide technical support and guidance for action on all plan and ordinance amendments and on applications for development approval and shall perform such other functions as may be
sources.full_text: (a) ESTABLISHMENT OF PLANNING AND COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT DEPARTMENT. The City Council hereby establishes and reaffirms the existence of the Planning and Community Development Department of the City of Suffolk. The Planning and Community Development Department shall perform the planning functions for the City and shall provide technical support and guidance for action on all plan and ordinance amendments and on applications for development approval and shall perform such other functions as may be requested by the City Council or authorized by this Ordinance. The Office of the Director of the Planning and Community Development Department "the Director" is hereby established and shall be responsible, with the Zoning Administrator, for administering the permitting functions of this Ordinance. The Director shall accept and act upon any subdivision plat or site plan pursuant to Code of Virginia, §§ 15.2-2258 to 15.2-2261 and this Ordinance. As used in this Ordinance, "Director" shall include any staff authorized by the Director to perform any function assigned to the Director by this Ordinance or Virginia law. (b) ESTABLISHMENT OF OFFICE OF ZONING ADMINISTRATOR. The City Council hereby establishes the Office of the Zoning Administrator "the Administrator." The Administrator is charged with the authority and the duty to administer and enforce this Ordinance. The Administrator is authorized to establish standards of operation and procedures for the Division of Community Development consistent with the intent of this Ordinance, and is further empowered to delegate the duties and powers granted to, and imposed upon, the Administrator by this Ordinance. As used in this Ordinance, "Administrator" shall include staff authorized by the Administrator to perform any function assigned to the Administrator by this Ordinance or Virginia law. The Administrator shall have the authority, powers and duties conferred by this Ordinance and by Code of Virginia, § 15.2-2286.4 and any and all additional authority, power and duties granted by Virginia law.

sources.title: SEC. 31-202. - BOARD OF ZONING APPEALS.
sources.excerpt: (a) ESTABLISHMENT. There is hereby established the Board of Zoning Appeals of the City of Suffolk. (b) APPOINTMENT. The Circuit Court shall appoint a Board of Zoning Appeals as provided in Code of Virginia, § 15.2-2308. (c) POWERS AND DUTIES. The Board of Zoning Appeals shall have the power, duty and authority to hear and decide variances, appeals, special exceptions, and map interpretations, and to exercise such other powers as may be granted by this Ordinance pursuant to Code of Virginia, §§ 1
sources.full_text: (a) ESTABLISHMENT. There is hereby established the Board of Zoning Appeals of the City of Suffolk. (b) APPOINTMENT. The Circuit Court shall appoint a Board of Zoning Appeals as provided in Code of Virginia, § 15.2-2308. (c) POWERS AND DUTIES. The Board of Zoning Appeals shall have the power, duty and authority to hear and decide variances, appeals, special exceptions, and map interpretations, and to exercise such other powers as may be granted by this Ordinance pursuant to Code of Virginia, §§ 15.2-2309 to 15.2-2312. Where the Board of Zoning Appeals has rendered a final decision relating to any application for development approval, it shall not consider any application which is substantially the same application for a period of one (1) year thereafter. (d) LOT DIMENSION WAIVERS. The Administrator may permit a modification in lot dimensions consistent with Sections 31-407(e)(2)B and 31-801 of this Ordinance without formal action by the Board of Zoning Appeals. The denial of a modification in lot dimensions by the Administrator may be appealed to the Board of Zoning Appeals in the manner prescribed by Section 31-309 of this Ordinance. Variance of yard requirements by more than 30 percent shall be obtained only through action of the Board of Zoning Appeals. (Ord. No. 10-O-081, § 1(Exh. A), 12-15-2010; Ord. No. 11-O-031, § 1(Exh. A), 5-18-2011)

sources.title: SEC. 31-203. - PLANNING COMMISSION.
sources.excerpt: (a) ESTABLISHMENT. Pursuant to Code of Virginia, § 15.2-2210, there is hereby established a planning body known as the Planning Commission of the City of Suffolk. (b) POWERS AND DUTIES. The Commission shall provide an advisory function to assist the City Council in making decisions pertaining to amendments to the Comprehensive Plan and this Ordinance, and applications for development approval. The Planning Commission shall render shall final decisions pertaining to applications for development a
sources.full_text: (a) ESTABLISHMENT. Pursuant to Code of Virginia, § 15.2-2210, there is hereby established a planning body known as the Planning Commission of the City of Suffolk. (b) POWERS AND DUTIES. The Commission shall provide an advisory function to assist the City Council in making decisions pertaining to amendments to the Comprehensive Plan and this Ordinance, and applications for development approval. The Planning Commission shall render shall final decisions pertaining to applications for development approval where such authority is assigned pursuant to this Ordinance. To that end, the Planning Commission shall have the following powers, duties and authority: (1) All of the powers, duties and authority assigned by Article 2 of Chapter 22 of Title 15.2 of the Code of Virginia. (2) To prepare and recommend for adoption a Comprehensive Plan, and any amendment to the existing Comprehensive Plan, pursuant to Code of Virginia, §§ 15.2-2223 to 15.2-2225, and 15.2-2227 to 15.2-2229. (3) To review the Comprehensive Plan once every five years pursuant to Code of Virginia, § 15.2-2230. (4) To render an initial determination as to whether the location, character and extent of any public facility subject to Code of Virginia, § 15.2-2232 is substantially in compliance with the Comprehensive Plan. (5) To prepare, recommend for adoption, recommend for amendment, and to review an official map for the location of established, future or proposed public streets, alleys, walkways, waterways, and public areas, pursuant to Article 4 of Chapter 22 of Title 15.2 of the Code of Virginia. (6) To prepare and recommend for adoption and amendment a subdivision ordinance (codified herein as Article 5) pursuant to Code of Virginia, §§ 15.2-2251 and 15.2-2253 and to administer the regulations as provided by Article 5 of this Chapter. (7) To accept and to act upon any subdivision plat or site plan pursuant to Code of Virginia, §§ 15.2-2258 to 15.2-2261 and this Ordinance where action has been referred by the Director in accordance with Article 5 of this Ordinance. (8) To accept and make recommendations to Council upon any application for approval of a conditional use permit. (9) To recommend for adoption zoning ordinances (codified herein as Articles 4 and 6—9) pursuant to Code of Virginia, § 15.2-2285. (10) Any and all additional authority, power and duties granted by Virginia law. (c) ORGANIZATION, MEMBERSHIP, TERMS, APPOINTMENT AND REMOVAL. The organization of the Planning Commission, and the membership, terms, appointment and removal of members of the Planning Commission, shall be as provided by Code of Virginia, §§ 15.2-2212 to 15.2-2217.

sources.title: SEC. 31-204. - CITY COUNCIL.
sources.excerpt: (a) POWERS AND DUTIES. The City Council shall render final decisions pertaining to amendments to the Comprehensive Plan and this Ordinance. The City Council shall render final decisions pertaining to applications for development approval where such authority is assigned pursuant to this Ordinance. The City Council shall have the following powers and duties: (1) To adopt the Comprehensive Plan and all elements of the Comprehensive Plan, pursuant to Code of Virginia, § 15.2-2226; (2) To adopt amen
sources.full_text: (a) POWERS AND DUTIES. The City Council shall render final decisions pertaining to amendments to the Comprehensive Plan and this Ordinance. The City Council shall render final decisions pertaining to applications for development approval where such authority is assigned pursuant to this Ordinance. The City Council shall have the following powers and duties: (1) To adopt the Comprehensive Plan and all elements of the Comprehensive Plan, pursuant to Code of Virginia, § 15.2-2226; (2) To adopt amendments to the text and map of the Comprehensive Plan, pursuant to Code of Virginia, § 15.2-2229; (3) To render a final determination as to whether the location, character and extent of any public facility subject to Code of Virginia, § 15.2-2232 is substantially in compliance with the Comprehensive Plan. (4) To appoint members of the Planning Commission and to provide for their compensation, consistent with the requirements of Code of Virginia, §15.2-2212. (5) To accept and/or appropriate funds for the expenses of the Planning Commission, pursuant to Code of Virginia, §§ 15.2-2217 and 15.2-2222. (6) To adopt, amend, and review an official map for the location of established, future or proposed public streets, alleys, walkways, waterways, and public areas, pursuant to Article 4 of Chapter 22 of Title 15.2 of the Code of Virginia. (7) To adopt and amend subdivision regulations (codified as Article 5 of this Chapter) pursuant to Code of Virginia, §§ 15.2-2240, 15.2-2251, and 15.2-2253, and to administer the subdivision regulations as provided in Code of Virginia, § 15.2-2255. Nothing in this section shall prohibit the City Council from delegating its authority to administer the subdivision regulations pursuant to Code of Virginia, § 15.2-2255 or as otherwise provided by Virginia law. (8) To accept and act upon any application for approval of a conditional use permit. (9) To rule upon petitions to restrict ingress and egress to a subdivision pursuant to Code of Virginia, § 15.2-2267. (10) To rule upon proposed plans and specifications for the installation of utilities within a subdivision pursuant to Code of Virginia, § 15.2-2269. (11) To render decisions as to whether to consent to the vacation of an interest, or to adopt an ordinance vacating an interest, in a site plan pursuant to Code of Virginia, § 15.2-2270 or to a subdivision plat pursuant to Code of Virginia, § 15.2-2271 or 15.2-2272. (12) To adopt zoning ordinances (codified herein as Article 4 and 6—9) pursuant to Article 7 of Chapter 22 of Title 15.2, Code of Virginia, including Code of Virginia, § 15.2-2285. (13) To appoint members of the Historic Landmarks Commission pursuant to Section 2-323 of the Code. (14) And all additional authority, power and duties granted by Virginia law.

sources.title: SEC. 31-205. - HISTORIC LANDMARKS COMMISSION.
sources.excerpt: (a) DESIGNATION. Pursuant to Code of Virginia, § 15.2-2306, the City Council hereby designates the Historic Landmarks Commission established pursuant to Division 7 of Article III of Chapter 2 of the City Code as the review board to administer the Historic Conservation Overlay Districts (Section 31-413 of this Ordinance). (b) POWERS AND DUTIES. The Historic Landmarks Commission shall exercise the powers set forth in Code of Virginia, § 15.2-2306 and any and all additional authority, power and dut
sources.full_text: (a) DESIGNATION. Pursuant to Code of Virginia, § 15.2-2306, the City Council hereby designates the Historic Landmarks Commission established pursuant to Division 7 of Article III of Chapter 2 of the City Code as the review board to administer the Historic Conservation Overlay Districts (Section 31-413 of this Ordinance). (b) POWERS AND DUTIES. The Historic Landmarks Commission shall exercise the powers set forth in Code of Virginia, § 15.2-2306 and any and all additional authority, power and duties granted by Virginia law.

sources.title: SEC. 31-206. - WETLANDS BOARD.
sources.excerpt: (a) DESIGNATION. Pursuant to Code of Virginia, § 28.2-1303. the City Council hereby establishes the Wetlands Board. (b) POWERS AND DUTIES. The Wetlands Board shall have the duties prescribed by Article VIII of Chapter 34 of the City Code and Section 31-418 of this Ordinance.
sources.full_text: (a) DESIGNATION. Pursuant to Code of Virginia, § 28.2-1303. the City Council hereby establishes the Wetlands Board. (b) POWERS AND DUTIES. The Wetlands Board shall have the duties prescribed by Article VIII of Chapter 34 of the City Code and Section 31-418 of this Ordinance.

sources.title: SEC. 31-301. - GENERAL PROCEDURAL REQUIREMENTS.
sources.excerpt: (a) APPLICATION PROCESS AND OFFICIAL FILING DATE. The specific procedures followed in reviewing various applications for development approval differ. Reference shall be made to the appropriate section in this Ordinance which addresses the procedures and requirements of a particular application. Generally, the procedures for all applications have three common elements: (1) submittal of a complete application, including required fee payment along with appropriate information; (2) review of the sub
sources.full_text: (a) APPLICATION PROCESS AND OFFICIAL FILING DATE. The specific procedures followed in reviewing various applications for development approval differ. Reference shall be made to the appropriate section in this Ordinance which addresses the procedures and requirements of a particular application. Generally, the procedures for all applications have three common elements: (1) submittal of a complete application, including required fee payment along with appropriate information; (2) review of the submittal by appropriate staff, agencies and boards; and (3) action to approve, approve with conditions, or deny the application. Submittal dates are established by the requirements of the specific application. (1) PRE-APPLICATION CONFERENCE (OPTIONAL). The applicant may, but is not required to, meet with the Director (in the case of legislative or administrative permits) or the Administrator (in the case of ministerial permits) to discuss the nature of the proposed application, application submittal requirements, the procedure for action and the standards for evaluation of the application. (2) APPLICATION MATERIALS. Current application materials shall be made available in the Office of the Director and the Office of the Administrator. Such applications shall be filed in advance of any public hearing or public meeting required pursuant to this Ordinance or the Code of Virginia. The Director may establish a schedule for filing any application for development approval requiring action by the Planning Commission or the City Council, which schedule shall provide adequate time for notice and/or publication consistent with Code of Virginia, § 15.2-2204 or other applicable requirements. Completed applications shall be filed according to any published schedule of the Planning and Community Development Department. (b) DECISIONMAKING ENTITIES. The agencies, officials, agents and/or employees of the City, as set forth in column (C) of the following Table 301-1, shall be designated as the agency with authority to render a determination as to the approval, approval with conditions or denial (the "Decisionmaker") on the applications for development approval set forth in column (A). The agencies, officials, agents and/or employees of the City, as set forth in column (B) of the following Table 301-1, shall be designated as the agency with authority to submit a recommendation relating to the application for development approval to the final decisionmaker. TABLE 301-1 (A) DEVELOPMENT ORDER (B) RECOMMENDING AGENCY (C) DECISIONMAKER Ministerial permits (1) Administrator Comprehensive Plan Amendment Planning Commission (2) City Council Zoning Amendment Planning Commission (2) City Council Conditional Rezoning Planning Commission (2) City Council Conditional Use Permit Planning Commission (2) City Council Preliminary Site Plan (conditional use or rezoning) Planning Commission (2) City Council Preliminary Site Plan (other) Department of Planning and Community Development, Department of Public Works, Department of Utilities, and Department of Parks, Recreation and Facility Management Director Final Site Plan Director Preliminary Subdivision Plat (Article 5) Director Final Subdivision Plat, Minor (Article 5) Director Final Subdivision Plat, Major (Article 5) Director Final Subdivision Plat, Family Transfer (Article 5) Planning Commission Variances, Appeals - Zoning Administrator Board of Zoning Appeals Variances - Subdivision Planning Commission City Council Appeals - Subdivision Planning Commission City Council Notes: (1) Other provisions of this Ordinance may authorize or require the Decisionmaker to refer the application for review and comment by other agencies. (2) The Director is responsible for receiving and submitting a staff report relating to the application for development approval prior to the formal recommendation or decision, as applicable. (c) PLANNING COMMISSION. The Planning Commission shall hold regularly scheduled public hearings to receive and review public input on those items required by this Ordinance. Where the Planning Commission is the decision maker, it shall approve, approve with conditions or deny an application for development approval. On those items where it has review authority, but is not the decision making agency, the Planning Commission shall recommend that the City Council approve, approve with conditions or deny applications. (d) CITY COUNCIL. The City Council shall hold regularly scheduled public hearings to act upon all items where it is the decision maker. The City Council shall decide whether or not to approve, approve with conditions or deny an application. (e) NOTICE PROVISIONS. The notice requirements for each type of application for development approval are prescribed in the individual Sections of this Article and Code of Virginia, § 15.2-2204 or as otherwise provided by law. In addition to notice of public hearings as required generally by law, the applicant for any land use public hearing shall erect on or immediately adjacent to such property a sign or signs as specified herein, giving public notice of the zoning action requested. One sign shall be erected so as to be visible and legible to each abutting public street. Where the property has extensive road frontage, one sign shall be erected for each two thousand (2,000) feet of frontage. When a property has no frontage on a public street, a sign shall be posted adjacent to the nearest public street from which future access is contemplated. Such signs shall be erected not less than fourteen (14) days before any public hearing of which they give notice and removed by the applicant within two (2) days after the City Council public hearing. Such signs shall be required for each advertised public hearing at which the application is considered. It shall be unlawful for any person to pull down, write on, cut or otherwise injure or deface such public notice. The applicant shall be responsible for the maintenance or replacement of signs obliterated or destroyed during the posting period. (f) SCOPE OF ACTION. The reviewing body may take any action on the application that is consistent with the notice given, including approval of the application, conditional approval of the application or denial of the application. The reviewing body may allow amendments to the application if the effect of the amendments is to allow a lesser change than that requested on the original application or to reduce the impact of the development or to reduce the amount of land involved from that indicated in the notices of the hearing. The reviewing body shall not, in any case, permit a greater amount of development, or a use falling in a different general use category, or a larger land area than indicated in the original application, or a greater variance than was indicated in the notice. (g) ADMINISTRATIVE PUBLIC HEARINGS PROCEDURES. The procedures set forth in subsection 31-301(h) shall apply to administrative public hearings, including public hearings on an appeal to the Board of Zoning Appeals. (1) SETTING OF THE HEARING. When the Administrator determines that a development permit application is complete, the Administrator shall cause notice of such hearing to be completed upon payment of any appropriate fee established pursuant to Section 31-111 of this Ordinance. (2) PURPOSE OF HEARING. The purpose of a public hearing is to allow the applicant and all other interested parties a meaningful and fair opportunity to be heard, to present evidence relevant to the application and to rebut evidence presented by others. All hearings under this Section shall be open to the public. Hearings shall be conducted in an impartial manner. Hearings shall be conducted according to the procedures set out in this Section, except to the extent and in the particulars that specific hearing procedures and time standards are governed by another statute or Section hereof. (3) CONDUCT OF HEARING. The conduct of a hearing before the Planning Commission, the Board of Zoning Appeals or the City Council shall be as set forth in their rules of procedure or as otherwise specified by state law, the City Code or the City Charter. (4) ADDITIONAL RULES. Where appropriate, additional rules governing the public hearing may apply including, but not limited to, other provisions of the Ordinance applicable to the body conducting the hearing and any of the body's adopted rules or procedures as long as the same are not in conflict with this Article. The body conducting the hearing may adopt rules of procedure to limit the number of applications for development approval which may be considered per meeting and to limit the time for each presentation, or each speaker. (h) LEGISLATIVE HEARINGS. (1) PURPOSE. The purpose of a legislative review public hearing is to provide the public an opportunity to be heard consistent with the adoption procedures provided by statute. Public hearings are required for legislative review hearings such as amendments to the Comprehensive Plan, amendments to the Unified Development Ordinance and the zoning map, and applications for a TND Overlay or a Planned Development rezoning. (2) NOTICE OF PROCEEDINGS. Notice shall be provided as set forth in Code of Virginia, § 15.2-2204 and Section 31-301(e) of this Ordinance. (3) CONDUCT OF THE HEARING. A. The order of the proceedings shall be as set forth in Section 2-95 of the City Code. B. Testimony may be presented by any member of the public, but need not be submitted under oath or affirmation. The decision-making body may establish a time limit for testimony. (4) RECORD OF PROCEEDINGS. The body conducting the hearing shall record the minutes of the proceedings by any appropriate means as prescribed by rule and consistent with Virginia law. (i) POST-DECISION PROCEEDINGS. (1) Any person, including any officer or agency of the City aggrieved by a final administrative determination on a development permit or administrative development approval by the Director or final decision-maker may appeal such final determination to the appellate body designated by this Article, if any, in the manner provided in this Section. (2) A written Notice of Appeal for permits required by this Article shall be filed within the period of time, and shall contain the information, specified by § 15.2-2311.A. The appeal shall be accompanied by the fee established by the City Council. If the appeal is from a Board of Zoning Appeals decision, it shall be filed within the period of time specified by Code of Virginia, § 15.2-2314. For ministerial permits, official map boundary or use interpretations, or notices of violation, the Notice of Appeal shall be filed with the Administrator. For conditional use permits, site plans, and subdivision plats, the notice of appeal shall be filed with the Director. (3) The procedure on appeal shall be governed by Code of Virginia, § 15.2-2312. (j) REVOCATION OF PERMIT OR APPROVAL. (1) APPLICABILITY. This Section shall apply to any situation where the applicant has supplied materially misleading information relating to the approval of an application for development approval. (2) DUTIES OF ADMINISTRATOR. If the Administrator determines that there are reasonable grounds for revocation of a development permit or approval, the Administrator shall set a hearing before the final decision maker. If the decision was made by the Director or the Administrator, the hearing shall be conducted by the Board of Zoning Appeals. If the City Council was the original decision-maker, it may refer the proposed revocation to the Planning Commission for a recommendation prior to the hearing. (3) NOTICE AND PUBLIC HEARING. Notice of the revocation hearing shall be given in the same manner as provided in Section 31-301, above. A public hearing shall be conducted in accordance with the procedures established in Section 31-301(h) of this Ordinance. (4) DECISION AND NOTICE. The decision maker shall render a decision to revoke the permit, to allow the applicant to retain the development permit, or to reconsider the permit. (5) EFFECT. A decision to revoke a development permit shall become final 30 days after the date the decision is rendered, unless appealed. After such effective date of revocation, any activities continuing pursuant to such permit shall be deemed to be in violation of this Ordinance. (6) RIGHT CUMULATIVE. The right to revoke a development permit, as provided in this Section, shall be cumulative to any other remedy allowed by law. (k) NEIGHBORHOOD MEETINGS. It is the policy of the City of Suffolk that any dispute between an applicant and another person that involves the person's rights, duties, or privileges should be settled through informal procedures. Accordingly, applicants for development approval involving a legislative or administrative permit are encouraged, but not required, to conduct meetings with neighborhood representatives, homeowner's associations, and other interested parties in order to resolve any pertinent issues prior to filing an application for development approval. (Ord. No. 19-O-061, § 2(Exh. B), 7-17-2019; Ord. No. 24-O-38, § 2(Exh. B), 4-17-2024)

sources.title: SEC. 31-302. - MINISTERIAL PERMITS.
sources.excerpt: The purpose of this Section is to prescribe procedures for permits which do not require administrative or legislative notice or a public hearing. A public hearing is not required for permits set forth in this Section for one or more of the following reasons: • Public hearings have already been conducted relating to the permit application, and the permit application procedure is designed to ensure that the proposed use complies with a previously approved subdivision plat, site plan, specific plan
sources.full_text: The purpose of this Section is to prescribe procedures for permits which do not require administrative or legislative notice or a public hearing. A public hearing is not required for permits set forth in this Section for one or more of the following reasons: • Public hearings have already been conducted relating to the permit application, and the permit application procedure is designed to ensure that the proposed use complies with a previously approved subdivision plat, site plan, specific plan, Comprehensive Plan amendment, or conditional rezoning (e.g., zoning permit, certificate of occupancy). • The proposed use is permitted by right in the applicable zoning district (e.g., zoning permit, certificate of occupancy). • The proposed use is subject to expedited review in order to avoid an unconstitutional prior restraint on speech (e.g., adult businesses, signs) or because of federal law (e.g., telecommunications permits). • The proposed use does not create an impact on public facilities and service, or otherwise has limited impacts on the affected neighborhoods, especially if development occurs in accordance with the standards set forth in this Ordinance (e.g., fence permits, home occupations). Exhibit 302-1 Ministerial Approval Review Process (a) APPLICABILITY. (1) No development or development activity may be undertaken within the City unless all development permits applicable to the proposed development are issued in accordance with the provisions of this Ordinance. At a minimum, no development or development activity may occur until a zoning permit has been obtained from the Administrator and a building permit is obtained from the Division of Community Development. Permits are required for all development, unless otherwise exempted, to ensure compliance with the various applicable codes, standards and laws and to ensure consistency with the Comprehensive Plan and policies of the City. (2) The procedures for approval of a Comprehensive Plan amendment, Zoning Ordinance amendment or rezoning, conditional use permit, or a site plan are prescribed in Sections 31-303 through 31-309 of this Article. The procedures for approval of permits which follow the above-referenced procedures, or permits for special situations, are set forth in this Section. Unless otherwise provided in any particular Section herein, the following procedure shall apply to the issuance of any permit required pursuant to this Section: A. The applicant shall submit the required materials to the Director as set forth in Appendix B of this Ordinance. Only complete applications shall be accepted. B. After the application is filed, the Director shall send a summary of review comments to the applicant. The applicant shall respond to any review comments. The application shall be approved, approved with conditions or denied. C. The filing of an appeal to the Board of Zoning Appeals shall toll any time period established herein for the approval or issuance of a permit required by this Section. D. Upon approval of any permit required by this Section (excluding Subsection (b) hereto, below), a zoning permit application may be submitted by the applicant pursuant to Subsection (b) of this Section. (3) The procedures for approval of a Board of Zoning Appeals matter are prescribed in Sections 31-303 through 31-309 of this Article. Unless otherwise provided in any particular Section herein, the following procedure shall apply pursuant to this Section: A. The applicant shall submit the required materials to the Administrator as set forth in Appendix B of this Ordinance. Only complete applications shall be accepted. B. After the application is filed, the Administrator shall send a summary of review comments to the applicant. The applicant shall respond to any review comments. The application shall be approved, approved with conditions or denied. C. The filing of an appeal to the Board of Zoning Appeals shall toll any time period established herein for the approval or issuance of a permit required by this Section. D. Upon approval of any variance, a zoning permit application may be submitted by the applicant pursuant to Subsection (b) of this Section. (b) ZONING PERMIT AND BUILDING PERMIT. (1) APPLICABILITY. A. On and after the effective date of adoption of this Ordinance, structures shall be erected only on parcels of land that have been created in conformance with this Ordinance, except as may be otherwise provided by Article 8 of this Ordinance. No person shall construct any structure, use any land or change the use of any structure or land until a zoning permit has been obtained from the Zoning Administrator and a building permit has been obtained from the Building Official. B. NON-CONFORMING USES. A zoning permit may be granted for a non-conformity only if the change in use is to a principal use permitted in the district, except as may be otherwise provided by Article 8 of this Ordinance. Once the change from a non-conformity to a principal permitted use is completed, no conversion to the previous non-conforming use shall be permitted. (2) PROCEDURES. A. Application. A zoning permit application is available from the Division of Community Development. If the proposed development or development activity is exempt from site plan review pursuant to Section 31-307 hereto, a plot plan (Appendix B, Section B-7) shall be filed for review along with the zoning permit application. If site plan or subdivision plat approval is required, the approved site plan and/or subdivision plat shall be presented with the application for a zoning permit. B. Completeness review and decision. The Administrator shall determine whether the application is complete. If the application is not complete, the Administrator shall return it to the applicant with a written explanation of the deficiencies. If the application is complete, the Administrator shall approve, approve with conditions or deny the application for a zoning permit. Applications which are denied shall have the reasons for denial, in writing, attached to the application. (3) APPROVAL CRITERIA. The following criteria shall be used to evaluate the zoning permit and building permit application: A. The building plans and related diagrams and written information shall be provided in conformance with the Uniform Statewide Building Code. B. The proposed building plans shall be in conformance with the land uses permitted in the zoning district in which the development project will be constructed. C. The building plans shall be in conformance with all applicable regulations of this Ordinance and the conditions of any unexpired subdivision plat, site plan or conditional use permit applicable to the subject property. D. Construction of required public improvements. (i) Prior to the acceptance of any public improvement by the City, the applicant shall provide the Director with sufficient testing data and certifications to determine that the improvements required by a site plan or subdivision plat have been properly constructed as depicted on the approved plan and to the standards prescribed by the City or other agency accepting the improvement. For single-family homes within an approved subdivision, the applicant shall, in lieu of the testing data and certification required above, provide the Director with a reference to the final plat for the lot subject to the zoning permit application. The cost of all testing and certification shall be borne by the applicant. (ii) The City shall be furnished with permanent, blackline, reproducible record drawings of public improvements constructed. E. A site plan showing lot grading must be submitted with the zoning/building permit application for residential use. This plan must be in accordance with the drainage plan approved by the City. In the event that previously approved drainage plans do not exist, plans shall be prepared in accordance with the lot grading standards established by the Department of Public Works. (4) SCOPE. A. A zoning permit authorizes the applicant to apply for and/or receive a building permit or an erosion and sedimentation control permit. A zoning permit shall not be deemed to authorize construction or development unless and until any required building permit or erosion and sedimentation control permit has been lawfully issued. B. The zoning permit shall be valid for the use for which the zoning permit was granted, as long as the use is in compliance with applicable codes, providing that construction commences within two (2) years of issuance of the zoning permit. A new zoning permit and any applicable fee shall be required for any approved project that did not commence construction within that two (2) year period. (c) SIGN PERMIT. The following regulations apply generally to all signs and are in addition to the regulations contained elsewhere in this Section: (1) REQUIRED; APPLICATION; INSPECTION OF SIGNS. No sign, unless herein excepted, shall be erected, constructed, structurally altered or relocated, except as provided in these regulations, until a zoning permit has been issued by the Administrator. Before any permit is issued, a preapplication with the Administrator, together with three sets of drawings or specifications, one to be returned to the applicant, as such may be necessary, to advise and acquaint the Administrator or his designee fully with the location of the sign in relation to adjacent buildings, construction, materials, manner of illuminating or securing or fastening, and number of signs applied for and the wording of the sign or advertisement to be carried on the sign. The alteration of sign faces by painting or overlay shall be considered as construction of a new sign and shall require a permit. Touching up or repainting existing letters or symbols shall be considered maintenance and repair and shall not require a permit. An application form and a site plan shall be submitted to the Administrator. (2) PROCESSING PROCEDURES. A. The Administrator shall review the application in accordance with the criteria established in the sign regulations of this Ordinance. The Administrator shall solicit review comments in accordance with general review procedures. B. The Administrator shall render a decision as to any sign permitted by right in the zoning district within 30 days after a complete application is submitted; provided, however, that permanent signs for planned unit developments, subdivisions and conditional uses shall be reviewed as part of the site plan approval process. C. In addition to the required zoning permit, all necessary building permits must be secured prior to the erection, structural alteration, rehanging or replacing of any sign or outdoor display structure. D. All signs shall be erected on or before the expiration of six (6) months from the date of issuance of the permit; otherwise the permit shall become null and void and a new permit shall be required. E. All signs, except those exempted under Subsection 31-714(b), shall require zoning permits. (3) APPROVAL CRITERIA. All signs shall be constructed and maintained in accordance with the Sign Regulations, Section 31-714. Sign types shall be those allowed for specified purposes in the zoning district in which the sign shall be erected. All signs shall be located on the premises to which they refer unless permitted as off-premise signs under this regulation. (4) SCOPE. A. The sign permit shall constitute the zoning permit for any sign. All signs shall be erected on or before the expiration of six months from the date of issuance of the permit; otherwise the permit shall become null and void and a new permit shall be required. B. In addition to the required zoning permit, all necessary building permits must be secured prior to the erection, structural alteration, rehanging or replacing of any sign or outdoor display structure. (d) HOME OCCUPATION PERMIT. (1) APPLICATION. No home occupation shall be erected, constructed or established unless and until a home occupation permit has been issued by the Administrator. (2) PROCESSING PROCEDURES. The procedure for processing a home occupation permit shall be as set forth in Subsection 31-302(b) of this Article. (3) APPROVAL CRITERIA. No home occupation permit shall be issued or approved, and no home occupation shall be established, maintained or operated, except in conformance with the home occupation regulations, Section 31-707, of this Ordinance. (4) SCOPE. The home occupation permit shall constitute the zoning permit for any home occupation subject to this Section. (e) TELECOMMUNICATIONS PERMIT. (1) APPLICATION. No antenna or tower shall be erected, constructed or established unless and until a telecommunications permit has been issued by the Administrator. (2) PROCESSING PROCEDURES. The procedure for processing a telecommunications permit shall be as set forth in Subsection 31-302(b) of this Article, except as follows: A. Where an antenna or tower is a permitted use, the Administrator shall review the application for development approval and submit his review comments to the applicant within 30 days after the application is submitted. The applicant shall respond to any review comments. B. Where an antenna or tower is a conditional use, the Director shall refer the application to the Planning Commission. The Director shall review the application, and submit a staff report to the Planning Commission. The matter shall be scheduled at the first available meeting of the Planning Commission but shall comply with any statutory requirements. The Planning Commission shall submit its recommendation within 60 days after its initial public hearing on the application. If no recommendation is timely submitted by the Planning Commission, the application shall be referred to the City Council without a Planning Commission recommendation, and shall be placed on the agenda of the City Council at the first available opportunity. The City Council shall render its decision approving, approving with conditions or denying the application for a conditional use permit within 60 days after the first public hearing relating to the application. (3) APPROVAL CRITERIA. No telecommunications permit shall be issued or approved, and no antenna or tower shall be constructed or erected, except in conformance with Section 31-716 of this Ordinance. (4) SCOPE. The telecommunications permit shall constitute the zoning permit for any telecommunications facility subject to this Section. (f) TEMPORARY USE PERMIT. (1) APPLICATION AND USES PERMITTED. Applicants for a temporary use permit shall submit a completed application form which shall contain sufficient information necessary to determine compliance with Section 31-717 of this Ordinance, and any other applicable Section of this Ordinance, as established by the Administrator. The uses listed in Section 31-717 are authorized temporary uses subject to the regulations and time periods specified therein and only in the zoning districts specified in Section 31-406 of this Ordinance. Unless otherwise specified herein, Temporary Use Permit renewals or extensions shall not be permitted. All other uses shall be considered permanent and subject to all regulations within this and other applicable Ordinances. (2) PROCEDURES. A. The applicant shall submit the required materials to the Administrator as set forth in Appendix B of this Ordinance which shall include a general project report and a site plan, except that the site plan format may be 11″ × 17″. Only complete applications shall be accepted. B. The Administrator shall review the application in accordance with the criteria established in Section 31-717 of this Ordinance. The Administrator shall solicit review comments in accordance with general review procedures. (3) CONDITIONS OF APPROVAL. In the allowance of such a use, the Administrator or, upon appeal, the Board of Zoning Appeals shall have authority to impose such reasonable conditions as necessary to protect the public health, safety and general welfare and to ensure that the use, value and qualities of the neighborhood surrounding the proposed location will not be adversely affected. (4) SCOPE. The temporary use permit shall constitute the zoning permit for any temporary use subject to this Section. The temporary use permit shall be valid for the use for which the permit was granted for the length of time indicated in the permit as long as the use is in compliance with applicable law. (g) CERTIFICATE OF OCCUPANCY. (1) APPLICATION. No building shall be occupied or used (including change of use) until a certificate of occupancy has been issued by the Building Official. A. Residential lots shall be graded to within 0.1' of the final grade prior to issuance of a Certificate of Occupancy (C.O.). In addition the lot grading shall provide a minimum positive slope of 1.0% for the lot, a minimum fall of six (6) inches within the first ten (10) feet from the building for pervious surfaces (lawns, landscaping, etc.), or with the current edition of the Virginia Residential Code (VRC) and a minimum fall of 2% slope away from the building on all non-covered impervious surfaces (excluding landings at exit doors, and covered porches) in the direction indicated on the approved plan. All Infill Development identified as: the development of small, scattered vacant sites which are surrounded or essentially surrounded by existing development and which because of location, configuration, access requirements, adjacent development patterns, or similar characteristics, may necessitate special consideration during the development process and shall require a predevelopment survey and post surveyor certification from a Certified Land surveyor (CLS) confirming this lot grading. In lieu of the certification by a land surveyor on non-infill lots, the builder may sign a certification stating that the approved lot grading meets the standards noted above. If this certification is found to be inaccurate, the City may discontinue the allowance for Builder/Owner certification and impose the requirement that future certifications must he from a Licensed Surveyor. For good cause shown, the Director may, upon recommendation from the Public Works Director or his designee, modify this standard provided that the proposed final grade does not negatively impact the approved overall drainage plan for the development and is certified by a professional engineer or land surveyor that the change provides positive drainage around the lot. B. Deficiencies found within the first year of the C.O. issuance must be corrected by the builder within sixty (60) days of notification. In the event the builder fails to correct the deficiencies, the City reserves the right to withhold additional permits, inspections, or C.O.'s until the lot grading is corrected on the referenced structure. The City may grant exceptions or accept surety on a case by case basis under extenuating circumstances. (2) PROCESSING PROCEDURES. A. The applicant shall submit the required materials with a request for final inspection, to the Administrator. B. The Administrator shall review the permit application and site plan submittal and require that the departments involved in the initial review make a final inspection to ensure that the work activities have been completed in accordance with the criteria established in this Ordinance and the applicable building and fire codes. C. A temporary certificate of occupancy may be issued by the Building Official for a period not exceeding six (6) months during alterations or partial occupancy of a building pending its completion in accordance with general rules or regulations applicable to such temporary certificate and with such additional conditions and safeguards as are necessary in the circumstances of the case to protect the safety of occupants and the general public. Such certificates may be renewed for periods not exceeding six (6) months. (3) APPROVAL CRITERIA. A. The Administrator shall find that all requirements of all permits issued for the development project have been satisfactorily completed. B. The Administrator shall obtain from the Building Official a statement that the final development conforms with the building permit issued for the development project. (4) SCOPE. The certificate of occupancy shall authorize the use and occupancy of a site, building or structure consistent with the terms and conditions of the certificate of occupancy and this Ordinance. (Ord. No. 13-O-117, 12-18-2013; Ord. No. 18-O-130, § 2(Exh. B, 12-19-2018; Ord. No. 19-O-030, § 2(Exh. b), 4-17-2019)

sources.title: SEC. 31-303. - COMPREHENSIVE PLAN AMENDMENT.
sources.excerpt: The purpose of this Section is to prescribe uniform procedures for the amendment of the Comprehensive Plan. Where the existing Comprehensive Plan does not provide sufficient densities, or where the themes, policies and actions do not support a proposed development, these procedures may be used to apply for an amendment to the plan. A "specific plan" for the development of property, which provides a bridge between the plan policies and specific regulations, may be approved to permit mixed use and
sources.full_text: The purpose of this Section is to prescribe uniform procedures for the amendment of the Comprehensive Plan. Where the existing Comprehensive Plan does not provide sufficient densities, or where the themes, policies and actions do not support a proposed development, these procedures may be used to apply for an amendment to the plan. A "specific plan" for the development of property, which provides a bridge between the plan policies and specific regulations, may be approved to permit mixed use and planned development such as a PD option development, TND option development or the designation of additional Village Center (VC) zoning districts. (a) APPLICATION. (1) Before any application is made, the applicant shall schedule a preapplication conference with the Director to discuss, in general, the procedures and requirements for a Comprehensive Plan amendment request pursuant to these regulations. (2) A property owner or his/her designated representative may initiate a Comprehensive Plan amendment. The applicant may combine an application for an amendment to the Comprehensive Plan with an application for approval of a rezoning, and said applications may be processed concurrently. (b) PROCEDURES. (1) INITIATION. A. An application for a Comprehensive Plan amendment shall be filed, with the filing fee, with the Director and shall contain the information set forth in Appendix B, Section B-3 of this Ordinance. Exhibit 303-1 Comprehensive Plan Amendment Approval Process The Planning Commission, the City Council, the Planning Director, a property owner or the owner of any business located in the City may initiate a request for an amendment to the Comprehensive Plan. The application for amendment may be accompanied by an application for a zoning district map amendment. By resolution, the City Council may establish a schedule prescribing when and how frequently Comprehensive Plan text amendments will be considered. (2) COMPLETENESS REVIEW. The Planning Director shall review the Comprehensive Plan amendment application and shall determine if the application is complete pursuant to this Section. The Director shall inform the applicant of the status of the completeness of the application. If the Planning Director determines the application is not complete, then the application shall be returned to the applicant along with the requisite fees. The applicant shall be instructed as to the reasons for the incompleteness of the application and informed of the schedule for the next application period. (3) PUBLIC HEARING AND DECISION. A. The Commission shall hold a public hearing and shall render its decision in accordance with the procedures set forth in Code of Virginia, §§ 15.2-2225, 15.2-2227, and 15.2-2229. B. The City Council shall hold a public hearing and shall render its decision in accordance with the procedures set forth in Code of Virginia, §§ 15.2-2226 and 15.2-2229. (c) APPROVAL CRITERIA AND FINDINGS. In determining whether the proposed amendment shall be approved, the Planning Commission shall consider the factors set forth in Code of Virginia, § 15.2-2223. (d) SCOPE. The approval of an amendment to the Comprehensive Plan shall not authorize the use, occupancy or development of property. The approval of a plan amendment shall authorize the applicant to apply for zoning changes and/or subdivision or site plan approval consistent with the themes, actions and policies of the Comprehensive Plan amendment. (Ord. No. 25-O-053, § 2(Exh. B), 5-21-2025)

sources.title: SEC. 31-304. - ZONING AMENDMENT AND REZONINGS.
sources.excerpt: (a) PROCEDURES. (1) INITIATION. A. Amendments to the zoning map including district boundaries or classifications may be initiated by: (i) The City Council by resolution; or (ii) The Planning Commission; or (iii) A petition properly signed and filed by the owner or, with the owner(s specific written consent, a contract purchaser or owner's agent. B. Whenever the City Council or Planning Commission shall initiate an amendment, either to the map or text, the public purposes for such an amendment sh
sources.full_text: (a) PROCEDURES. (1) INITIATION. A. Amendments to the zoning map including district boundaries or classifications may be initiated by: (i) The City Council by resolution; or (ii) The Planning Commission; or (iii) A petition properly signed and filed by the owner or, with the owner(s specific written consent, a contract purchaser or owner's agent. B. Whenever the City Council or Planning Commission shall initiate an amendment, either to the map or text, the public purposes for such an amendment shall be clearly stated. C. When an amendment to the Official Zoning Map (a "rezoning"), or an amendment to the text of this Ordinance, is initiated, an application for such amendment shall be submitted to the Director. Before any application is accepted by the Director, the applicant may attend a preapplication meeting with the Director or designee. The purpose of the preapplication meeting is to discuss, in general, the procedures and requirements for a zoning amendment request pursuant to this Ordinance. (2) COMPLETENESS REVIEW. A. A request for an amendment of the text of the Ordinance or a rezoning shall be made by filing a written request and application with the Director. The applicant shall file the information required by Appendix B, Section B-4 to this Ordinance for a rezoning application. B. The Director shall review the application for a rezoning or text amendment for completeness. If the application is complete, the Director shall schedule the application for a public hearing with the Planning Commission. If the application is not complete, the Director shall return the application to the applicant with a statement of the reasons for the deficiency. C. Upon certification by the Director that the application is complete and payment of required fees, the application shall be deemed received and referred to the Planning Commission for its review and recommendation as provided by Code of Virginia, § 15.2-2204. (3) PUBLIC HEARING AND DECISION. A. The Planning Commission, after public notice in accordance with Code of Virginia, § 15.2.2204 and Section 31-301(e) shall hold at least one public hearing on such application and as a result thereof shall transmit a recommendation to the City Council. A public hearing shall be conducted, and a recommendation shall be submitted, by the Planning Commission in accordance with the requirements of Section 31-301(h) of this Ordinance and Code of Virginia, § 15.2-2285.A and 15.2-2285.B. After receipt of the recommendations of the Planning Commission, the City Council shall approve, approve with conditions, or deny the rezoning or text amendment in accordance with Code of Virginia, § 15.2-2285.C. B. When a rezoning application is accompanied by an application for a conditional use permit or subdivision approval such dual applications may be processed and reviewed concurrently. If the proposed rezoning is inconsistent with the Comprehensive Plan, an application for an amendment to the Comprehensive Plan shall be submitted by the applicant in accordance with Section 31-303 of this Ordinance. Amendments to both the official zoning map and the Comprehensive Plan may be considered concurrently. (b) APPROVAL CRITERIA. The Planning Commission and City Council shall consider the matters set forth in Code of Virginia, § 15.2-2284 in drawing and applying zoning districts and in considering amendments to this Ordinance. Furthermore, adequate utilities, access roads, fire protection, and other necessary facilities shall be provided as set forth in Section 31-601 of this Ordinance. (c) SUBSEQUENT APPLICATIONS. No application for change of zoning of any lot, parcel or portion thereof shall be considered by the City Council within one year of the final action of the City Council upon a prior application covering any of the same described land. This provision, however, shall not impair the right of the City Council to reconsider any rezoning request by motion. After a decision is made to reconsider a rezoning request, the City Council shall conduct a public hearing and may choose to act on the rezoning request without referring the matter to the Planning Commission for its recommendation. (d) SCOPE OF APPROVAL. The approval of an amendment to this Ordinance or a rezoning shall not be deemed to authorize the development of land. Such an amendment shall authorize the applicant to apply for a certificate of zoning permit, in the case of uses permitted as of right, or a conditional use permit, in the case of conditional uses. Such amendment does not supersede any requirement for site plan or subdivision plat approval by this Ordinance. (e) PROCEDURES FOR RECORDING ZONING MAP AMENDMENTS. When the amendment involves changes to the existing zoning district boundaries, the form of the amending Ordinance shall contain a narrative description of the land to be reclassified or reference to an accompanying plat of such land showing the new zoning classifications and indicating their boundaries. The Director shall refer to said attested Ordinance as a record of the current zoning status until such time as the zoning map can be changed accordingly. (Ord. No. 14-O-137, 12-17-2014)

sources.title: SEC. 31-305. - CONDITIONAL REZONING.
