The areas designated for tractor trailer truck stacking shall be shown on the approved site plan. (7) TRUCK ROUTING. A truck routing and signage plan shall be supplied by the proposed facility with the site plan for review and approval by the City. The plan shall detail all truck routes entering and leaving the facility and all roads within the City of Suffolk to be used by trucks utilizing the facility until the trucks are on the nearest major arterial. The plan shall include a signage plan and shall provide details of methods to be utilized to keep trucks on the approved routes. The proposed facility shall be responsible for the costs to supply and install the required on-site signage. (8) LANDSCAPING BUFFERS. Within the required setbacks, landscaping buffers shall be provided in accordance with the following standards. Where a berm is provided to mitigate noise, no additional landscaping shall be required other than that listed in Sec. 31-726(B)(3). A. PERIPHERAL. 1. A minimum 20-foot wide landscaped buffer shall be provided along the front property line and along all abutting roadways with breaks for approved access points. 2. A minimum 15-foot wide landscape buffer shall be planted along all other property lines where the use abuts adjacent public property, residential uses, institutional uses, or recreational uses, or property zoned as such. 3. No parking, loading, unloading, coupling/uncoupling of trucks, storage, trash storage or compaction, or other operations shall be permitted within these required buffer areas. B. REQUIRED PLANTINGS. Nine (9) canopy trees plus thirteen (13) understory trees plus fifty-five (55) evergreen shrubs per 100 linear feet of buffer shall be planted in the buffer. Plant material shall meet the size standards of the Unified Development Ordinance. C. EXISTING VEGETATION. Existing healthy, well-formed canopy trees, understory trees, and shrubs may be credited towards the requirements of this section, provided: 1. The vegetation meets the minimum size standards of the Ordinance; and 2. The vegetation is protected before and during all phases of development of the site. Tree protection fencing shall be erected at least one linear foot outside the tree's dripline; and 3. The vegetation is maintained thereafter in a healthy growing condition. (9) WAIVER OF SETBACK REQUIREMENTS. A. WAIVER. For parcels located in industrial parks (as defined in Appendix "A", Definitions of the Unified Development Ordinance) existing as of September 1, 2023 or for expansion of existing industrial uses, the Director shall have the authority to waive requirements of this section relating to setbacks. The Director shall provide a written determination that such waiver of setback standards demonstrates the following: 1. The strict application of setback standards result in improvements that would be incompatible to the development that could be reasonably anticipated on land surrounding the property; and 2. The strict application of setback standards result in improvements that would create an undue burden that would not be expected of surrounding property upon development; and 3. The waiver will not result in any substantial adverse impacts to adjacent property and or the character of the area in which the property is located; and 4. The waiver will not be detrimental to the public health, safety, and general welfare of the community; and 5. The need for a waiver is created by the physical character of the property, including dimensions and topography, or by other extraordinary circumstances or condition of such property, or by the use or development of property immediately adjacent thereto. Personal, self-inflicted or financial burden shall not be considered as grounds for the issuance of a waiver; and 6. The need for a waiver is unique in that it is not so general or recurring in nature as to make reasonably practicable the formulation of general regulations to be adopted as an amendment to the Ordinance; and 7. Waivers will not conflict with the intent of the Ordinance or the intent of the City's adopted Comprehensive Plan. B. WAIVER SUBMITTAL REQUIREMENTS. Waiver requests must be submitted in writing and must clearly outline which section(s) of the Ordinance for which a waiver is being requested. Applicants must clearly demonstrate the need for said waiver by providing documentation in support of the request. Requests may be made prior to or at the time of site plan submittal. (10) All performance standards, as identified in Section 31-608 of the Unified Development Ordinance, shall be met. (Ord. No. 23-O-146, § 2(Exh. B), 11-15-2023)

sources.title: SEC. 31-727. - TRUCK STOPS AND TRAVEL PLAZAS.
sources.excerpt: (a) PURPOSE. The purpose of this section is to ensure the compatibility of truck stop and travel plaza uses with the surrounding areas. These regulations establish requirements for the design and installation of these facilities in order to safeguard public safety, health, and welfare of the City, and minimize impacts to adjacent properties. (b) APPLICABILITY. These standards shall apply to all facilities and uses that provide any fueling lanes for tractor trailer trucks. (c) PERFORMANCE STANDAR
sources.full_text: (a) PURPOSE. The purpose of this section is to ensure the compatibility of truck stop and travel plaza uses with the surrounding areas. These regulations establish requirements for the design and installation of these facilities in order to safeguard public safety, health, and welfare of the City, and minimize impacts to adjacent properties. (b) APPLICABILITY. These standards shall apply to all facilities and uses that provide any fueling lanes for tractor trailer trucks. (c) PERFORMANCE STANDARDS. Truck stops and travel plazas shall meet the following minimum standards: (1) Facilities shall provide tractor trailer truck parking in the amounts shown in Table 727-1. TABLE 727-1 3 or fewer truck fueling lanes with no truck scale More than 3 truck fueling lanes with no truck scale 3 or fewer truck fueling lanes with a truck scale More than 3 truck fueling lanes with a truck scale Minimum number of Tractor Trailer Truck Parking Spaces 4 truck spaces per fueling lane 5 truck spaces per fueling lane 6 truck spaces per fueling lane 10 truck spaces per fueling lane (2) Access to the tractor trailer truck parking spaces shall be available during all hours that the facility is open to the public. (3) Each tractor trailer truck parking space shall have a minimum width of twelve (12) feet and a minimum length of sixty (60) feet. (4) Truck chassis and shipping containers shall not be parked or stored on the site without an attached truck cab. (5) The tractor trailer truck parking shall comply with the following standards: (i) Parking spaces for tractor trailer trucks and truck cabs shall be constructed with a paved surface of heavy duty asphalt or concrete that is certified by a professional engineer to support the anticipated load. (ii) The parking spaces shall be provided on the same parcel as the principal structure. (iii) The individual spaces shall be marked with paint or other appropriate markings. (iv) All drive aisles shall be paved with asphalt or concrete, and shall be maintained free of potholes. (6) Any repair of vehicles on the site shall only occur in a truck repair facility operated with the facility. No repair of trucks shall occur in the parking areas. (7) A 20-foot wide vegetative landscape screen shall be provided around the perimeter of the site where it abuts any public property, and properties containing residential uses, institutional uses, conservation uses, or recreational uses, or property zoned as such. Screening shall be consistent with Section 31-603(f) of the Unified Development Ordinance. In cases where the facility abuts residential development, a 6 foot tall, opaque fence shall also be placed along the property line between the facility and the residential properties. (8) All tractor trailer trucks parked on the site must remain in an operable condition and be roadworthy with all required vehicle licenses and current tags. (9) At least one tractor trailer truck stacking space shall be provided for each tractor trailer truck fueling lane. Stacking spaces shall have a minimum width of twelve (12) feet and a minimum length of sixty (60) feet. (10) In conjunction with the Conditional Use Permit or site plan application (as applicable), an AutoTURN or other appropriate swept path analysis exhibit demonstrating adequate turning space and movement for vehicles on the site shall be submitted. (11) Accessory uses associated with the facility may include sleeping accommodations for crews of commercial vehicles or trucks, sale of parts and accessories for automobiles, commercial vehicles, or trucks, truck repair facilities, truck scales, restaurants, convenience stores, gasoline sales, recreational vehicle septic pump-out facilities, car washes, and truck washing facilities. (12) Tractor trailer trucks utilizing the facility may be parked on the site overnight with the facility operator's consent. (13) Parking for passenger vehicles shall be provided in accordance with Section 31-606 of the Unified Development Ordinance and shall be physically separated from the truck parking and fueling areas by the placement of islands or other appropriate physical barriers. (14) All performance standards, as identified in Section 31-608 of the Unified Development Ordinance, shall be met. (Ord. No. 25-O-064, § 2(Exh. B), 6-18-2025)

sources.title: SEC. 31-801. - INTENT.
sources.excerpt: (a) Within the districts established by this Chapter or amendments that may later be adopted, there exist lots, structures, uses of land and structures, and characteristics of use which were lawful before this Chapter was passed or amended, but which would be prohibited, regulated or restricted under the terms of this Chapter or future amendments. It is the intent of this Chapter to permit these nonconformities to continue until they are removed, but not to encourage their survival. It is furthe
sources.full_text: (a) Within the districts established by this Chapter or amendments that may later be adopted, there exist lots, structures, uses of land and structures, and characteristics of use which were lawful before this Chapter was passed or amended, but which would be prohibited, regulated or restricted under the terms of this Chapter or future amendments. It is the intent of this Chapter to permit these nonconformities to continue until they are removed, but not to encourage their survival. It is further the intent of this Chapter that nonconformities shall not be enlarged upon, expanded or extended, nor be used as grounds for adding other structures or uses prohibited elsewhere in the same district. (b) Nonconforming uses are declared by this Chapter to be incompatible with permitted uses in the districts involved. A nonconforming use of a structure and land in combination shall not be extended or enlarged after passage of this Chapter by attachment on a building or premises of additional signs intended to be seen from off the premises, or by the addition of other uses, of a nature which would be prohibited generally in the district involved. (c) To avoid undue hardship, nothing in this Chapter shall be deemed to require a change in the plans, construction or designated use of any building on which actual construction was lawfully begun prior to the effective date of adoption or amendment of this Chapter and upon which actual building construction has been carried on diligently. Actual construction is hereby defined to include the placing of construction materials in permanent position and fastened in a permanent manner. Where excavation or demolition or removal of an existing building has been substantially begun preparatory to rebuilding, such excavation or demolition or removal shall be deemed to be actual construction, provided that work shall be carried on diligently. (d) The Zoning Administrator shall make determinations of the nonconforming status of uses within sixty (60) days of receipt of the request unless the requester has agreed to a longer period. The request shall contain, at a minimum, a written statement requesting the nonconforming status of land. It shall be incumbent upon the owner of the property containing the use to provide the Zoning Administrator satisfactory evidence as to the nonconforming status of the land use in question. The Zoning Administrator also may gather and utilize evidence or request additional evidence as necessary to make a determination. The determination shall be in writing and shall be subject to appeal to the Board of Zoning Appeals pursuant to Code of Virginia § 15.2-2311. (e) NONCONFORMING LOTS OF RECORD. (1) SINGLE NONCONFORMING LOTS OF RECORD. Permitted uses in any district may be allowed on any single lot of record at the effective date of adoption or amendment of this chapter, notwithstanding limitations imposed by other provisions of this chapter. Nonconforming lots created by judicial partition after the effective date of this chapter shall be eligible for a building permit if they meet the requirements of Section 31-801(e)(1)B except the ability of the Administrator or Board of Zoning Appeals to authorize a modification shall include minimum lot size and lot width. Such lot must be the subject of an approved resubdivision in accordance with Article 8, Section 31-801(e)(2), Nonconforming Lots of Record with Common Frontage below. The dimensional and density requirements established in Article 4, Section 31-407, Dimensional and Density Regulations shall apply even though such lot fails to meet the requirements for lot area or lot width, or both, generally applicable in the underlying zoning district except as provided for as follows: A. Within the RL, RLM, RM, RC, and RU zoning districts the Zoning Administrator may authorize or require an adjustment to and reestablishment of the minimum and maximum (if applicable) required front and side yard setbacks as follows: (i) FRONT YARD SETBACK. The front yard setback of new single family detached or semi-attached construction shall be equal to the average front yard setback distance established by the existing houses on the same block or within the same neighborhood; (ii) SIDE YARD SETBACK. The side yard setback of new single family detached or semi-attached construction shall be as generally applicable for the underlying zoning district and may be adjusted and reestablished to a distance not to exceed a modification of thirty percent (30%) of the generally applicable required side yard setback; (iii) The need to authorize the adjustment and reestablishment of any required front or side yard setback shall depend upon a written finding by the Zoning Administrator that such adjustment and reestablishment: a. Is reasonably necessary for the practical use of the lot; b. Is reasonably necessary to maintain the architectural character, conformity, and development patterns of the surrounding neighborhood and developed properties; c. Will not have substantial adverse effects on adjacent property or the surrounding neighborhood; d. The width of any individual dwelling shall not be less than twenty (20) feet; e. When determining the need for such adjustment and reestablishment in any required yard dimension the Zoning Administrator shall consider: • The conformity and similarity of the mass, bulk and building foot print of any proposed new construction to the mass, bulk and building foot print of the majority of existing houses on the same block or within the same neighborhood; • The conformity and similarity of the front yard setback or distances between buildings and the front property line of any proposed new construction to the average front yard setback or distance between buildings and the front property line of existing buildings on the same block or within the surrounding neighborhood. Unless otherwise necessary for the practical use of the lot, the front yard setback of new construction shall be equal to the average front yard setback distance established by the existing buildings on the block or within the same neighborhood; • The conformity and similarity of the side yard setback or distances between buildings and the side property line of any proposed new construction to the average side yard setback or distance between buildings and the side property line of existing buildings on the same block or within the surrounding neighborhood. Unless otherwise necessary for the practical use of the lot, the side yard setback of new construction shall be equal to the existing buildings on the block or within the same neighborhood. B. Within any zoning district the Zoning Administrator may authorize a modification of not to exceed thirty percent (30%) in the dimension of any required yard, including any required yard as reestablished pursuant to Article 8, Section 31-801(e)(1)(A), Single Nonconforming Lots of Record above, subject to the requirements of VC § 15.2-2286.4. Approval shall depend upon a written finding that such modification: (i) Complies with the requirements of VC § 15.2-2286.4; (ii) Is reasonably necessary for the practical use of the lot; and (iii) Will not have substantial adverse effects on adjacent property or the surrounding neighborhood. Any modification of the required front yard or side yard setback by more than thirty percent (30%) shall require a variance from the Board of Zoning Appeals. (2) NONCONFORMING LOTS OF RECORD WITH COMMON FRONTAGE. Where such parcel of continuous frontage lots within the RL, RLM, RM, RC, and RU zoning districts fronts on an existing public street or right-of-way the lot area, lot width and front and side yard setback requirements for any existing or resubdivided lot within such parcel of continuous frontage lots shall be as follows: A. LOT WIDTH. The minimum lot width of any approved new or resubdivided lot established under this provision shall be equal to the average lot width for the existing developed lots on the same block or within the same neighborhood. The minimum lot width of any approved new or resubdivided lot established under this provision shall be thirty-five (35) feet and the lot width of any approved new or resubdivided lot established under this provision shall be within eighty-five percent (85%) of each other; B. FRONT AND SIDE YARD SETBACKS. The front and side yard setbacks of any approved new or resubdivided lot established under this provision shall be established in accordance with the requirements of Article 8, Section 31-801(e)(1), Nonconforming Uses, Intent, Nonconforming Lots of Record, Single Nonconforming Lots of Record above. C. Approval of such division or resubdivision of land shall be contingent upon a written finding by the Subdivision Agent that: (i) Such division or resubdivision of land will result in the creation of not more than a total of four (4) separate lots (consisting of three (3) new lots plus the residual of the parent parcel); (ii) Is reasonably necessary to maintain the architectural character, conformity, and development patterns of the surrounding neighborhood and developed properties; and (iii) Will not have substantial adverse effects on adjacent property. D. Appeals of the decision of the Subdivision Agent shall be processed in accordance to Article 5, Section 31-515, Subdivision, Variances and Appeals. (f) NONCONFORMING USE OF LAND, LAND AND STRUCTURE IN COMBINATION. (1) Nonconforming use of land, or of land and structures in combination, may be continued subject to the following regulations, so long as such use remains otherwise lawful: A. STRUCTURAL CHANGE. No existing structure devoted to a use not permitted by this Chapter in the district in which it is located shall be enlarged, extended, constructed, reconstructed, structurally altered or moved except in changing the use of the structure to a use permitted in the district in which it is located, except as provided in Subsection E. of this Section. B. EXTENSION OR EXPANSION. Any nonconforming use may be extended throughout any parts of a building which were manifestly arranged or designed for such use at the time of adoption or amendment of this Chapter creating the nonconformity, but no such use shall be extended to occupy any land outside such building. No nonconforming use of land outside buildings, or involving no buildings, shall be enlarged, increased or extended to occupy a greater area of land than was occupied at the time the use became nonconforming, or moved to any portion of the lot or parcel not occupied by such nonconforming use at such time. C. CHANGES IN USE. Any nonconforming use may be changed to a use conforming to district regulations for the district in which it is located. If a Conditional Use Permit is obtained, any nonconforming use may be changed to another nonconforming use. Any change in use shall be permitted only in accord with applicable district regulations on off-street parking, off-street loading and signs; and such Use Permit shall be granted only upon a finding that the proposed use, with such conditions and safeguards attached, would be equally or more appropriate to the surroundings than the existing nonconforming use. Once a change has been made in nonconforming use, no change shall thereafter be made reverting to the original nonconforming use. D. DISCONTINUANCE. If a nonconforming use is discontinued for more than two (2) years (except when government action impedes access to the premises, in which case the period of interruption shall exclude the time during which government action impeded access), the structure, land or structure and premises in combination in nonconforming use shall not thereafter be used except in conformity with the regulations of the district in which it is located. E. DAMAGE OR DESTRUCTION. A nonconforming use which is damaged or destroyed by a cause beyond the control of the owner may be reestablished or reconstructed if the project is commenced within one year of the date of such damage or destruction, and diligently pursued to completion within two (2) years of the date of such damage or destruction; provided, however, that such reestablishment or reconstruction shall not have the effect of enlarging or extending the nonconforming use, unless in conformance with the provisions of this Article. After two (2) years, all nonconforming use rights shall be lost. Reconstruction of nonconforming structures shall be in accordance with the terms of this Article. If a nonconforming structure is damaged or destroyed by a natural disaster or other act of God, such building shall be repaired, rebuilt or replaced to eliminate or reduce the nonconforming features to the extent possible, without the need to obtain a variance. If such building cannot be repaired, rebuilt or replaced except to restore it to its original nonconforming condition, the owner shall have the right to do so. The owner shall apply for a building permit and any work done to repair, rebuild or replace such building shall be in compliance with the provisions of the Uniform Statewide Building Code and any work done to repair, rebuild or replace such building shall be in compliance with the provisions of the local flood plain regulations adopted as a condition of participation in the National Flood Insurance Program. Unless such building is repaired or rebuilt within two (2) years of the date of the natural disaster or replaced within two years of the date of the disaster or other act of God such building shall only be repaired, rebuilt or replaced in accordance with the provisions of the Zoning Ordinance. However, if the nonconforming building is in an area under a Federal Disaster Declaration and the building has been damaged or destroyed as a direct result of conditions that gave rise to the declaration, then the Zoning Ordinance shall provide for an additional two (2) years for the building to be repaired, rebuilt or replaced as otherwise provided in this paragraph. F. USE PERMIT USES NOT NONCONFORMING. Any existing use which would be permissible by use permit in the district in which it is located shall without further action be deemed conforming except for the class of uses involving change from one nonconforming use to another. G. EXPANSION OF USE OR STRUCTURE IN THE CHESAPEAKE BAY PRESERVATION AREA OVERLAY DISTRICT. Expansion of existing uses and structures located in the Chesapeake Bay Preservation Area Overlay District shall be subject to provisions of Section 31-415 of the City Code, in addition to the provisions of this Article. In the case of conflict between the provisions contained in Section 31-415 and those contained in this Article, the more restrictive provisions shall apply. H. NONCONFORMING MANUFACTURED HOUSING UNIT. Nothing in this Section shall be construed to prevent removal of a valid nonconforming manufactured housing unit from property and replacement of that unit with another comparable manufactured housing unit that meets the current HUD manufactured housing code. Such replacement unit shall retain the valid nonconforming status of the prior unit. (2) NONCONFORMING STRUCTURES. Where a lawful structure becomes nonconforming by reason of adoption or amendment of this Chapter and could not be built under the terms of this Chapter by reason of restrictions on area, lot, coverage, height, location on the lot, yards or other requirements concerning the structure (exclusive of its use), such structure may be continued so long as it remains otherwise lawful, subject to the following provisions: A. STRUCTURAL CHANGE, EXTENSION OR EXPANSION. No structure or portion there shall be enlarged or structurally altered in any way which increases its nonconformity, but any structure or portion thereof may be altered to decrease its nonconformity. B. DAMAGE. A nonconforming structure which is damaged or destroyed by a cause beyond the control of the owner may be reestablished or reconstructed if the project is commenced within one year of the date of such damage or destruction, and diligently pursued to completion within two (2) years of the date of such damage or destruction; provided, however, that such reestablishment or reconstruction shall not have the effect of enlarging or extending the nonconforming structure, unless in conformance with the provisions of this Article. After two (2) years, all nonconforming use rights shall be lost. Reconstruction of nonconforming structures shall be in accordance with the terms of this Article. If a nonconforming structure is damaged or destroyed by a natural disaster or other act of God, such building shall be repaired, rebuilt or replaced to eliminate or reduce the nonconforming features to the extent possible, without the need to obtain a variance. If such building cannot be repaired, rebuilt or replaced except to restore it to its original nonconforming condition, the owner shall have the right to do so. The owner shall apply for a building permit and any work done to repair, rebuild or replace such building shall be in compliance with the provisions of the Uniform Statewide Building Code, and any work done to repair, rebuild or replace such building shall be in compliance with the provisions of the local flood plain regulations adopted as a condition of participation in the National Flood Insurance Program. Unless such building is repaired or rebuilt within two (2) years of the date of the natural disaster or replaced within two years of the date of the disaster or other act of God such building shall only be repaired, rebuilt or replaced in accordance with the provisions of the Zoning Ordinance. However, if the nonconforming building is in an area under a Federal Disaster Declaration and the building has been damaged or destroyed as a direct result of conditions that gave rise to the declaration, then the Zoning Ordinance shall provide for an additional two (2) years for the building to be repaired, rebuilt or replaced as otherwise provided in this paragraph. (3) MOVING. Should any such nonconforming structure, or portion of structure, be moved for any reason, and for any distance whatever, it shall thereafter conform to the regulations for the district in which it is located after it is moved. (4) REPAIRS AND REPLACEMENT. On any nonconforming structure or portion of structure, and on any structure containing a nonconforming use, work may be done on ordinary repairs, or on repair or replacement of nonbearing walls, fixtures, wiring or plumbing, provided that: A. The cubic content of the nonconforming structure or portion shall not be increased; and B. Nothing contained in this Section shall prevent the strengthening or restoring to safe condition of a building or part thereof declared to be unsafe by any official charged with protecting the public safety, upon order of such official. The structure may be restored or replaced to the same or lesser size and in the same location. The nonconforming use may be resumed and continued as before or on a lesser scale, but shall not be enlarged or intensified. The restoration or replacement must be equally or more appropriate to the surroundings than the existing nonconforming structure. (Ord. No. 11-O-031, § 1, 5-18-2011; Ord. No. 13-O-117, 12-18-2013; Ord. No. 19-O-030, § 2(Exh. B), 4-17-2019; Ord. No. 19-O-092, § 2(Exh. B), 9-18-2019; Ord. No. 23-O-077, § 2(Exh. B), 6-21-2023)

sources.title: SEC. 31-802. - NONCONFORMING CHARACTERISTICS OF USE.
sources.excerpt: If characteristics of use, such as signs, off-street parking and loading, lighting or other matters pertaining to the use of land, structures and premises, are made nonconforming by this Chapter, as passed or amended, no change shall be made in such characteristics of use which increases nonconformity with district or other regulations of this Chapter, but change may be made which decreases such nonconformity. (a) NONCONFORMING AGRICULTURAL USES. Notwithstanding any other provisions of this Chap
sources.full_text: If characteristics of use, such as signs, off-street parking and loading, lighting or other matters pertaining to the use of land, structures and premises, are made nonconforming by this Chapter, as passed or amended, no change shall be made in such characteristics of use which increases nonconformity with district or other regulations of this Chapter, but change may be made which decreases such nonconformity. (a) NONCONFORMING AGRICULTURAL USES. Notwithstanding any other provisions of this Chapter to the contrary, except as provided for in Article 7, Section 31-702, the use of land or any parcel thereof for agricultural purposes as a nonconforming use shall not prevent or prohibit the repair, total replacement, restoration or, with a Conditional Use Permit, the additional construction thereof of such barns, shelters, sheds, tenant houses or other buildings or structures necessary or desirable for agricultural purposes, including the raising or maintaining of swine, cattle, poultry or other fowl.

sources.title: SEC. 31-803. - SPECIAL EXCEPTIONS FOR THE EXPANSION OF NONCONFORMING USES.
sources.excerpt: (a) ENUMERATION: REQUIREMENTS FOR ISSUANCE. The Board of Zoning Appeals shall hear and decide on applications for such special exceptions as may be authorized in this Chapter. No such special exception may be granted except after notice and hearing as provided by Code of Virginia, § 15.2-2204. The Board may impose such conditions relating to the use for which a permit is granted as it may deem necessary in the public interest, and may require a guarantee or bond to ensure that the conditions imp
sources.full_text: (a) ENUMERATION: REQUIREMENTS FOR ISSUANCE. The Board of Zoning Appeals shall hear and decide on applications for such special exceptions as may be authorized in this Chapter. No such special exception may be granted except after notice and hearing as provided by Code of Virginia, § 15.2-2204. The Board may impose such conditions relating to the use for which a permit is granted as it may deem necessary in the public interest, and may require a guarantee or bond to ensure that the conditions imposed are being and will continue to be complied with. (b) PERMITTED SPECIAL EXCEPTIONS. The following special exceptions may be permitted by the terms of this Chapter. (1) A nonconforming use now existing in any part of a building to be extended vertically or laterally to other portions of the building. In a building now occupied by a nonconforming commercial or industrial use, an additional use of the same classification in the remainder of the building. (2) Erection of an additional building upon a lot occupied by a nonconforming use on the effective date of this Ordinance, by a nonconforming commercial or industrial establishment and of which additional building is part of such establishment. (3) The extension or alteration of a building being used for a legal nonconforming use upon the lot occupied by such building on the effective date of this Ordinance. (c) REQUIRED ARRANGEMENTS PRIOR TO ISSUANCE. Before any special exception shall be issued, the Board shall make written findings certifying that satisfactory provision and arrangement has been made concerning the following: (1) Ingress and egress and proposed structured thereto, with particular reference to automotive and pedestrian safety and convenience, traffic flow and control, and access in case of fire or catastrophe. (2) Off-street parking and loading areas, where required, and the economic, noise, glare or odor effects of the special exception on adjacent properties and properties generally in the district and the vicinity. (3) Refuse and service areas. (4) Utilities, with reference to locations, availability and compatibility. (5) Screening and buffering with reference to type, dimensions and character. (6) Signs, if any, and proposed exterior lighting, with reference to glare, traffic safety, economic effect and compatibility and harmony with properties in the district. (7) Required yards and other open space. (8) General compatibility with adjacent properties and other property in the district, and shall further make written certification that, in the board's opinion, the special exception will not substantially adversely affect the use of adjacent and neighboring properties permitted by this Chapter.

sources.title: SEC. 31-804. - VESTED RIGHTS DETERMINATION.
sources.excerpt: (a) PURPOSE. (1) To provide detailed administrative rules, regulations and procedures in order to guide officials in the administration, interpretation and implementation of the Comprehensive Plan, the Unified Development Ordinance, and/or any other Ordinances, regulations and or administrative rules adopted by the City in order to implement the Comprehensive Plan. (2) To establish predictability and fairness for affected landowners; (3) To recognize that development projects for which vested ri
sources.full_text: (a) PURPOSE. (1) To provide detailed administrative rules, regulations and procedures in order to guide officials in the administration, interpretation and implementation of the Comprehensive Plan, the Unified Development Ordinance, and/or any other Ordinances, regulations and or administrative rules adopted by the City in order to implement the Comprehensive Plan. (2) To establish predictability and fairness for affected landowners; (3) To recognize that development projects for which vested rights have been obtained must be accounted for in the Comprehensive Plan, the Unified Development Ordinance, Capital Improvements Programs, and other land development regulations. (4) To provide a method for determining and quantifying the number of projects, development projects, and land uses, which do not now comply with the UDO, or which may in the future fail to comply with the UDO due to subsequent amendments to the UDO but which are vested, so that such projects, development projects and land uses can be accounted for in the existing and future general plans and the UDO. (5) To establish uniform and non-burdensome procedures and specific criteria for the determination of vested rights and claims of equitable estoppel in order to aid in the accomplishment of sound and orderly planning; (6) To define the scope of vested rights that have been obtained by virtue of prior development approvals, including the expiration of development permits; (7) To protect legitimate investment-backed expectations; (8) To protect the planning and implementation process; (9) To settle potential disputes and to minimize protracted and costly litigation; (10) To facilitate implementation of the goals, objectives and policies set forth in the Comprehensive Plan; and (11) To ensure that all applicable legal standards and criteria are utilized in the determinations to be made hereunder. (b) MONITORING. (1) The Administrator shall be responsible for monitoring and maintaining records of the following: A. Applications for vested rights determinations. B. Applications for consent agreements. (2) Monitoring shall include: A. The name and location of the project; B. The date of application filing; C. The type of application submitted; D. The disposition of the application; and E. The reason for the disposition. (c) APPLICATIONS FOR VESTED RIGHTS DETERMINATION-PREREQUISITES. (1) No application for Vested Rights Determination shall be considered until an initial determination of completeness has been rendered by the Administrator. (2) If a Vested Rights Determination is not filed, no use shall be made of said property, nor shall any building or structure on said property be occupied, constructed, reconstructed, altered, enlarged, or repaired, nor shall any application for development approval be submitted or approved, except in conformity with the then-existing provisions of this Ordinance. (3) The failure of the Administrator to comply with any provision of this Section shall not excuse the property owner or person affected from filing an application for a Vested Rights Determination within the period prescribed by this Ordinance. (d) SUBMISSION REQUIREMENTS. An application for a Vested Rights Determination shall be made by the applicant on a form established for such purpose and provided by the Administrator and shall contain at least the following information: (1) Name and address of applicant; (2) Project description and name of subdivision or development, if applicable; (3) Location of development; (4) Total land area, in square feet; (5) Total area of impervious surface, in square feet; (6) Number of residential dwelling units, by type; (7) Type and amount of non-residential square footage; (8) Phases of the development, if applicable; (9) Verified or certified copies of all development permits, contracts, appraisals, reports, correspondence, letters, or other documents or materials upon which the applicant's claim for vested rights or equitable estoppel is based; (10) A sworn statement, in a form prescribed by the Administrator, and signed by the applicant; (11) A legal description of the property; and (12) Such other relevant information as may be reasonably required by the Administrator or the Council in order to ensure full and proper consideration of the application. (e) COMPLETENESS OF APPLICATION. (1) The Administrator shall not review an application for a Vested Rights Determination unless he has deemed that it is complete. (2) An application for a Vested Rights Determination shall not be deemed complete if any relevant information is not provided, if the application form is not signed by the applicant, if required documents have not been provided pursuant to Subsection (e) herein, or if other information, as may be specified by the Zoning Administrator and which is necessary to adequately review the application, is not supplied in a timely fashion. (3) The Administrator shall make an initial determination as to whether the application is complete. If the Administrator has determined that an application for a Vested Rights Determination is complete, the Administrator shall process the application in accordance with the procedures set forth herein. If the Administrator has determined that an application for a Vested Rights Determination is incomplete, the Administrator shall notify the applicant specifying the reasons for the determination of incompleteness. The Administrator shall take no further action until the deficiencies are corrected and the application is resubmitted or until the Council acts upon the Administrator's recommendation pursuant to Subsection (g) hereof. (4) A determination of completeness shall only constitute compliance with the submission requirements of this Section and the applicable application forms, and shall not imply compliance with the substantive requirements of this Section, nor shall it indicate that the information submitted by the applicant is accurate or has been verified or certified. The official policy of the City with regard to the completeness of an application filed hereunder shall be established solely by the City Council. (5) An applicant aggrieved with a decision of incompleteness by the Zoning Administrator pursuant to this Subsection may file an appeal to the Board of Zoning Appeals within the time period prescribed by Code of Virginia, § 15.2-2311. (f) DETERMINATION OF VESTED RIGHTS. (1) The Administrator shall prepare a decision as to whether a complete application for a vested rights determination should be approved, approved with conditions or denied. A decision shall require the written concurrence of the City attorney. Any appeals of the decision shall be forwarded to the Board of Zoning Appeals pursuant to Code of virginia, § 15.2-2309.3. (2) The Administrator's decision shall address the following, at a minimum: A. A determination of whether the applicant has been found to have acquired vested rights and the basis therefor; and B. If the order includes a determination that vested rights exist: (i) The geographic scope of the determination in relation to the total area of the development site; (ii) The duration of the determination; (iii) The substantive scope of the vested rights determined to have been acquired and the limitations applicable thereto; (iv) Any appropriate conditions, consistent with the rights of the applicant, which are needed to ensure consistency with the Comprehensive Plan and the UDO. (3) The Administrator's decision shall constitute the minimum determination needed to ensure that the applicant's vested rights are protected. (4) The decision shall be transmitted to the applicant in writing. (g) STANDARDS AND CRITERIA FOR VESTED RIGHTS DETERMINATION APPROVAL. (1) GENERALLY. A. The applicant has the duty and responsibility to demonstrate compliance with the standards and criteria set forth in this Section by a preponderance of the evidence. B. The standards and criteria set forth herein are not intended to limit the Administrator in applying the common law of vested rights or equitable estoppel, and the Administrator may, in any particular case, consider all such applicable law. (2) COMMON LAW VESTED RIGHTS. No Vested Rights Determination claiming entitlement to approval of an Application for Development Approval shall be approved or issued unless the applicant has demonstrated compliance with the criteria set forth in this Section for vested rights or the criteria set forth in this Section for equitable estoppel. A. For a claim of vested rights, the applicant shall demonstrate the following: (i) The applicant filed an application for development approval prior to the effective date of the amendment. Applications filed after the effective date of an amendment shall be presumed not to comply with this standard; and (ii) Said application was complete under the terms of the land development regulation pursuant to which said application was filed; and (iii) The application for development approval was made by the applicant and accepted by the City well in advance of the Amendment, and the City or the decisionmaking agency or official had an opportunity to require the applicant to submit relevant information; and (iv) The Application for Development Approval was issued or approved by an officer or agency with authority to render such approval; and (v) In reliance on the approval set forth in Subsection (iv) hereto, the applicant made substantial commitments or substantial obligations within the purview of the activities authorized by said permit or approval; and (vi) The applicant has proceeded in good faith, and no approvals or permits have lapsed or been revoked; and (vii) The applicant has established any other factor which may establish vested rights under state or federal law, including, but not limited to, Code of Virginia, § 15.2-2307. B. For a claim of equitable estoppel, the applicant shall demonstrate compliance with the following criteria: (i) The existence of an act or omission of an official or agency, beyond the mere failure to enforce an Ordinance or regulation, with authority to approve the application for development approval, upon which the applicant may rely. An "act" means an action that is clear, definite and affirmative. An "omission" means a negligent or culpable failure to act where there was a duty to act; and (ii) The actual, good faith reliance by the applicant on the act or omission described in Subsection (i), above; and (iii) That the applicant made substantial changes in position or incurred extensive expenses in reliance on the act or omission described in Subsection (i), above, beyond the mere purchase of the property. (h) CONSENT AGREEMENTS. Any applicant for a Vested Rights Determination may apply for Consent Agreement Approval provided that the requirements of Subsection (h) of this Section are satisfied or the required approval is for one (1) or more, but less than all phases of the proposed development. An application for Consent Agreement Approval may be approved subject to compliance with a consent agreement. (1) An Application for Consent Agreement Approval shall be processed in the same fashion as an application for a Vested Rights Determination as set forth in Subsection (g) of this Section. (2) In addition to the required processing as set forth above, an Application for Consent Agreement Approval shall include, but shall not be limited to the following: a timing and phasing plan for the proposed development; a plan for the provision of public facilities and services to the proposed development, by phase; the conditions under which the proposed development will be authorized to proceed; and the conditions under which approvals or permits will lapse or may be revoked. (3) Consent Agreements shall include all of the following terms and conditions: A. A legal description of the subject property and the names of the legal and equitable owners; B. The duration of the consent agreement and the conditions that will result in revocation; C. The uses permitted on the property, including population densities and/or building intensities and height; D. A description of the public facilities that will service the proposed development, including who shall provide such facilities; the date any new facilities, if needed, will be constructed; and a schedule to assure that public facilities are available concurrent with the impacts of the development; E. A description of any preservation or dedication of land for public purposes; F. A description of all development approvals, permits, or other local or state approvals needed for the proposed development; G. A finding that the proposed development is consistent with the general plan and the relevant provisions of the land development regulations; H. A description of any conditions, terms, restrictions, or other requirements determined to be necessary for the preservation and protection of the public health, safety, or welfare; I. A statement indicating that the omission of a limitation or restriction shall not relieve the applicant of the necessity of complying with all applicable local, state and federal laws; J. A phasing plan indicating the anticipated commencement and completion date of all phases of the proposed development; and K. A statement that the Administrator shall review progress pursuant to the consent agreement at least once every twelve (12) months to determine if there has been demonstrated good faith compliance with the terms of the consent agreement. If the Board of Zoning Appeals finds, on the basis of substantial competent evidence, that there has been a failure to comply with the terms of the consent agreement, the consent agreement may be revoked or modified by the Board of Zoning Appeals after a public hearing which has been noticed by publication, and for which notice has been expressly provided to the applicant.

sources.title: SEC. 31-805. - EXPIRATION OF DEVELOPMENT APPROVALS.
sources.excerpt: (a) TIME OF EXPIRATION. Unless otherwise specifically provided for in this Ordinance, development applications shall automatically expire and become null and void, and all activities taken pursuant to such development application shall cease and become null and void, and all activities pursuant to such approval thereafter shall be deemed in violation of this Ordinance, when: 1) the applicant fails to satisfy any condition that was imposed as part of the original or revised approval of the Applic
sources.full_text: (a) TIME OF EXPIRATION. Unless otherwise specifically provided for in this Ordinance, development applications shall automatically expire and become null and void, and all activities taken pursuant to such development application shall cease and become null and void, and all activities pursuant to such approval thereafter shall be deemed in violation of this Ordinance, when: 1) the applicant fails to satisfy any condition that was imposed as part of the original or revised approval of the Application for Development Approval, or that was made pursuant to the terms of any development agreement, including the failure to abide by specified time limits established therein; or 2) the applicant fails to present a subsequent development application as required by this title within the time so required or as may be required by Virginia law or Table 805-1 or 805-2 of this Ordinance. If no time limit for satisfaction of conditions is specified in the original or revised approval of the development application, the time shall be presumed to be 18 months from the date of approval. (b) EXTENSION PROCEDURES. Unless otherwise prohibited by Virginia law or this Ordinance, the decisionmaker may extend the time for expiration of a development permit or approval for a period not to exceed one (1) year from the date of the original decision granting approval, if the application for extension is made in writing within the original period of validity. Subsequent extensions may be made by the final approval body upon finding that conditions at the time of approval have not changed. (c) DATE FROM WHICH TIME LIMIT IS MEASURED. (1) Each time period referenced on Table 805-1 and Table 805-2 shall run from the final action of the appropriate official, officer, board, commission or the governing body with valid and legal jurisdiction to take such action or to approve such plans or to issue such permits. Except as may be otherwise indicated herein, the date of final action shall be the date such action was taken or such approval was granted or such permit was issued, as set forth on such action, approval or permit. (2) In order to assure that all applicants for actions, approvals or permits are informed of the applicable time limit, the date of final action and the expiration date shall be shown on each such action, permit or approval; but provided, however, that the Administrator's failure to include either the date of final action or the expiration date shall not be deemed to be a waiver of such dates nor shall it be the basis of any action by the applicant to challenge the applicable expiration date. The burden is on the applicant to know the date of issuance and the expiration date. If either or both of such dates are not shown, the applicant may request, and the Administrator shall promptly supply such date or dates. (3) All actions, approvals or permits shall expire on the expiration date unless a valid extension has been granted on or before the expiration date pursuant to Subsection (a) herein. (d) EXTENSIONS OF TIME LIMITS. (1) There shall be no extensions of any time limits for actions, approvals or permits set forth herein, as of right. Any extensions must be expressly requested by the applicant, in writing, and approved by the appropriate official, officer, board, commission or the governing body which originally took the action, approved the plan or issued the permit. (2) A request for an extension of an expiration date shall be made on a form provided by the Administrator and shall include, but shall not necessarily be limited to, the following: A. The current date of expiration; B. The extension period requested, which shall be no longer than the original period of time granted; and C. The reason(s) that the applicant has been unable to proceed within the period of the original expiration date. (3) Before granting an extension, the official, officer, board, commission or the governing body shall determine whether any applicable changes in land use regulations have occurred which would impose new requirements with respect to such action, approval or permit, if an extension were denied, and the applicant were compelled to refile for an original action, approval or permit. If changes have occurred, the official, officer, board, commission or the governing body shall balance the burden imposed on the applicant if required to refile for an original action, approval or permit against the benefit accruing to the public by requiring the applicant to comply with the new regulation. TABLE 805-1: TIME LIMITS FOR USE OF ZONING APPROVALS AND PERMITS Action/Permit/Approval Time Limit Zone change: Map or Text Change One year to obtain a building permit after site plan approval for a building or buildings comprising 25 percent of the building floor area of the site plan. Conditional Use Permit One year to obtain a building permit and commence construction of the primary use authorized by the permit or, in the case of home occupations, to complete any necessary alterations, adjustments, modifications or other activities authorized by the permit. Special Use Permit (Prior Zoning Ordinance) Variance or Special Exception Six months to obtain a building permit and to commence construction of the primary use authorized by the variance. Planned Development (Site Plan) One year to commence construction; two years from commencement of construction to complete construction. Certificate of Occupancy One year. TABLE 805-2: TIME LIMITS FOR USE OF SUBDIVISION OR SITE PLAN APPROVALS Action/Permit/Plat/ Plan/Approval Time Limit Preliminary Plat or Preliminary Site Plan One (1) year to submit final plat or preliminary plat void. One (1) year to submit final site plan or preliminary site plan void. Final Plat or Final Site Plan Five years to complete construction of improvements or, as otherwise determined, in accordance with Code of Virginia, § 15.2-2261 (see Section 31-509(g)(1) of this Ordinance).
